review_text: Bloodstained doesn’t quite recapture the magic of Castlevania, and doesn’t really expand on the formula in a meaningful way.  Biggest positives: + Most of the game’s atmosphere comes from the conspicuously detailed level backdrops + Landing a powerful shard attack is pretty fun  Other notes: - The mix of surface-deep systems may not be enough to keep the attention of all but the most ardent Castlevania fans - Animations and character models can dip into ‘mobile game’ territory  Full review: https://toothbrush.blog/review/bloodstained-ritual-of-the-night/

Al principio es muy bola, y algunos bosses son injustos (te quitan 1/3 de vida porque sí), pero cuando farmeas un rato, este juego te recompensa muy bien. El lore es asombroso y las referencias son god. La música es sublime y el diseño igual. Lo recomiendo jugar, por lo menos una run.

If you think the best way to pay homage to old games is to just make games like back in the day then you'll love this. It's pretty stylish and could be a great introduction to the castlevania genre (we don't give metroid any credit in this household). The story is predictable and pretty boring, more engaging than the early Belmont stories, but that's a very low bar to clear. The level design is fine but it gets repetitive by the end, especially since the true ending is tied to clearing a bunch of arbitrary optional roadblocks. The weapon diversity is pretty shallow so the RPG mechanics end up feeling underutilised. And the end game balancing is old school and spongey. In the end it's a decent fun game, it's just that every single aspect of it is predictable.

I like the game actually, but giving it a negative rating because there's a Epic sign-in popup right after you defeat the final boss of the game. That is horrendously inconsiderate. Please fix this - ruins the final moments of the game which should be some of the most memorable.  Beyond that, rather decent game. The story is not all that great (actually it might be the dialogue and writing that's pulling the story down - the overall arc is pretty decent even if predictable), and the visuals can be grating at times, but the game was really fun to explore. I had a lot of fun with the bosses - their designs and abilities were quite cool. The locations and music are also quite good. I haven't played all of the Igavania games (notably SOTN) and it was a long time ago, but this brought back memories for sure.  One gripe I'd like to note in particular is that there were multiple points in the game where I had to look up how to proceed. The problem wasn't that I didn't know where to go - I explored all over - but that each time player is supposed to do something unintuitive and it isn't telegraphed very well. Maybe I wasn't paying attention, but the writing in the game really doesn't incentivize that.  Overall, good game if you want to scratch the Castlevania itch. Be prepared to end up banging your head against the wall and eventually having to look up how to proceed more than once.

Very fun metroidvania, heavy on the -vania. It can be extremely grindy and repetitive if you want to get really strong so that you can experience any/all optional content, but that's up to you.  It took me the 27 hours you see here to 100% everything, but the main story was doable in ~10 hours. A lot of weapons and skills, cool abilities, fun areas, and satisfying treasures/cosmetics to collect. Beat up every wall, btw.

it doesnt have that much going for it over the castlevania series it exists to usurp, but it does a good job standing among those games as another solid entry. its like aria of sorrow with a bigger map and a crafting system. if those things sound cool to you, you'll like this game. if you want a game that advances the ideas those older games pioneered, you'll probably be disappointed. ritual of the night plays it very safe, and this safety is what i think holds this game back the most. it's ultimately just another game that plays like symphony of the night. if that isnt enough to tell you whether or not you'll like this game, you should try symphony of the night or aria of sorrow instead. the rest of this review will reflect the thoughts of someone who played this game way too ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ much and got herself thoroughly sick of it, so take them with a grain of salt:  the full 3d style reveals a whole lotta cracks that were never present in the old sprite-based castlevanias. though the imagination and talent put into each of the game's assets is clear, it feels as if every one of them was designed to be as appealing and eye-catching as possible in a vacuum, with little consideration for how all of these elements would fight for your attention the frame. the pieces the environments are made of are lavishly detailed and a feast for the eye whenever you stop to take a closer look, but the soft, bland lighting of most areas puts that detail to poor use. the game lacks a singular unifying "look" for each area's visual design to adhere to and subvert as it sees fit, and it results in a persistent feeling of drabness despite all the bright colors. the enemy designs, as lovingly rendered as each of them are in the bestiary, feel more like a grab bag of whatever monster concepts they could think of.  on top of all this visual dissonance is the straight up jank, particularly in cutscenes. clearly, the people making this game are very experienced making snappy and precise 2d action platforming gameplay, and not so skilled at making visually compelling scenes. the cutscene animations all throughout the game are laughably stilted and jarring, and the camerawork never does a good job of selling any scene well, usually showing the action from bland or confusing angles and rarely moving between those points in an interesting way. taken as is, it hardly matters, because you're not here for the storytelling. but watching cutscenes that look *this* bad does have a psychological effect on me, that makes me notice all the little imperfections present in gameplay that much more. its so strange, even when skipping all the cutscenes on repeat playthroughs, i still think about them and have it subtly affect my view of the rest of the game. it makes me that little bit less excited to listen to the game's repetitive voice lines and walk through its long hallways at just slightly too slow a speed for comfort. its weird ♥♥♥♥!  so yeah, cool game that does what its going for well, but its a package that will grate on you over time if you pay too close attention to it. most of the extra ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ they added after launch isnt worth playing either, just get a rom of castlevania 3 for the famicom like fr that game is SO ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ good

原汁原味的《恶魔城》啊，怪物使魔充满既视感，爷青回！  RPG元素加进来之后好玩多了，可以build了，可以打怪物弱点了[strike]（虽然意义不大）[/strike]，可以收集各种图鉴了[strike]（累死）[/strike]！ 不过也有小毛病： 比如缓慢的移速（装了饰品之后移速才正常好吗！）； 比如无感的剧情（什么xx就是幕后黑手?!……但是xx是谁来着？）； 比如没什么交互的战斗（平均被怪物肘飞十米）； 比如中期刮痧的boss战（凭什么主线没逃课还会卡数值）； 比如烦人的老太太（今天必须给你喂饱！） 我一度以为老太太的任务是引导我刷怪物升级，结果发现跑图半天就给个发型，不仅经验金币没涨，还因为买食材花光所有积蓄，亏爆了啊啊！  总的来说还是开心的，当成纯JRPG来玩了，银河战士味儿没那么重，当然地图设计还是不错的。 期待一下续作吧，也期待银河城类型能再次伟大，能有更多预算更大体量的银河城游戏啊。
如果你是恶魔城玩家，那么玩着个就对了，虽然角色建模没用那么好，后期地图有一些无聊，，而且操作有一些老（不过我要的就是那个味儿）但架不住确实是一款好游戏

Not a bad game, but too stiff and overloaded for me. I understand why fans of classic Castlevania enjoy Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night. It has a huge castle, many weapons, shards, crafting, secrets and that familiar Igavania structure. But for me, the game never became truly exciting. The combat feels very stiff and outdated. Hits have almost no impact: you attack, a short animation plays, a damage number appears, and that is basically it. Many fights feel less like real combat and more like clicking enemies until they disappear. The backstep is weak, movement feels limited, and double jump is almost the only thing that makes combat feel more mobile. The game also throws too much loot at you: weapons, armor, accessories, materials, recipes, food and shards. But a lot of it feels like inventory noise rather than meaningful build variety. Exploration often becomes checking rooms for another chest instead of discovering a world that feels truly interesting. Some progression gates are poorly hinted too. Using Blood Steal on the blood fountain is technically logical, but the game gives almost no clear sign that this old fountain is an important blocked path. That kind of design feels more like guessing than satisfying exploration. Compared to Hollow Knight or GRIME, Bloodstained lacks the same sense of movement, combat precision, atmosphere and meaningful discovery. Those games made me want to explore and master the combat. Bloodstained often made me feel like I was just clearing rooms and moving forward to finish the game. Also, the lack of official Ukrainian localization is disappointing. Ukrainian seems to exist only through an unofficial patch. I can see why old Castlevania fans love it, but for me it feels too wooden, overloaded and outdated. 6/10 — decent for one playthrough, but not really my thing.

It lacks polish in many ways: - Some bossfights are trivial while others range from cool (notably the first one,  the ||rematch|| and blood vampire) which have varied moves and different behaviour depending on what range you have from them, to lacking much forewarning on moves, pause between them (hitboxes overlap often) and  clear way to dodge them (the bouncy bug) to most annoyingly nigh-sitting ducks (the invert dragon).  - Too many shards are "summon X" and to my taste thats just uninspired. - The cooking system is genuinely cool, but the drop rates on certain items that are often required grinding which is just unfun, this would be solved with a better spread of monsters dropping certain items or better yet, a bigger map which would organically give you more to do and force you to fight more monsters so you get more drops. The idea of entering and exiting a room 20 times to get a lemon dropped is just not entincing. The same criticism extends to the armor/weapon system.  - Some enemies flinch way easy from damage (underwater horse) while others chage at you like a crackhead regardless of damage.  Game feel, and movement is in general good, animation cancelling attacks when landing with your jumping attack midway is satisfying and so is the dive kick but it is not exciting to play when the map is not that big or varied, enemies are mostly meh and bossfights are not always worth looking towards.

Bloodstained me chamou atenção justamente por conta dos desenvolvedores, Castlevania SoTN foi uma obra prima que marcou a sua geração, e marca até hoje, mas Bloodstained não.  O visual do jogo é maravilhoso eu não tenho como negar isso, a ideia de design é muito bem feita, porém o jogo só tem isso de interessante (isso se você desconsiderar os serrilhamentos ridículos que se mantém mesmo com anti-aliasing no máximo e o lip sync feito por um amador). A história é genérica, rasa, sem profundidade nenhuma, chata de acompanhar e extremamente previsível. O jogo não te dá nenhuma motivação pra continuar acompanhando a história e se você for acompanhar, como eu fiz, vai se decepcionar ainda mais.  A gameplay não é fluída, com hitboxs muito estranhas onde você consegue acertar ataques pelas paredes e tomar dano relativamente longe dos inimigos. Todas as armas dão a sensação de serem pesadas demais por conta do delay insuportável e o jogo não tem esforço nenhum para te dar uma mínima mudança de gameplay com cada arma, tudo parece que foi feito nas coxas.  A exploração é extremamente chata sem nenhuma sensação de recompensa e diversas salas vazias que não tem absolutamente nada, com idas e vindas gigantescas só por conta de você errar um único pulo, tudo nesse jogo dá a impressão que foi feito para comer o máximo possível de horas de quem joga. Isso poderia ser resolvido com uma disponibilidade maior de habilidades, mas não, metade do que você precisa para progredir está em drop de inimigos que o jogo não te dá nenhuma dica que seria necessário e a outra metade (tirando um ou outro) ficam no lugar de suas "magias", com você tendo que trocar toda hora só pra poder explorar.  Os inimigos tem padrões de ataques muito repetitivos e muitas vezes parece que foram feitos por uma criança, sem contar a inteligência dos inimigos que nem sequer deve ser chamada de inteligência. Sem falar na quantidade de inimigos que em certas áreas não tem nada e em outras áreas tem de monte, não faz sentido como eles distribuem os inimigos. Os Bosses são horríveis, padrões de ataques pequenos que se repetem, movimentação básica (ou nenhuma), pouca vida e ainda por cima teve vários casos de eu conseguir matar chefes literalmente ficando parado e apertando o botão de ataque. Sua trilha sonora é completamente esquecível, não consigo lembrar de um único momento onde eu realmente gostei da trilha sonora ou ela marcou uma área/chefe e os efeitos sonoros conseguem ser ainda piores, todo ataque a Miriam vai ficar gemendo, com a voz dela mais alta do que a trilha sonora. Os inimigos tem os mesmos efeitos sonoros desde o início até o final do jogo e isso inclui suas invocações, elas mantém os mesmos três efeitos sonoros padrão dos inimigos. Pra piorar: o jogo não tem save automático e se você morre e esqueceu de salvar? parabéns, vai ter que fazer tudo de novo. Aconteceram incontáveis vezes isso comigo, geralmente eu relevo, mas nesse jogo chegou num ponto de ser insuportável. Eu ainda estava tentando me forçar a fazer o final verdadeiro do jogo, mas para piorar o jogo crashou e voltou meu save.  TLDR: Esse jogo foi uma completa decepção, se alguém me falasse que isso foi feito por alguém que fez Castlevania eu não acreditaria, mas infelizmente foi feito. Tudo nesse jogo é um consumo de tempo desnecessário e uma falta de polimento ridícula. Eu peguei em promoção, mas o preço original (com as DLCS) fica mais de 200 reais. É um completo desrespeito aos fãs de Castlevania e a quem pagou esse preço absurdo.

Bloodstained is one of those "Fine, i´ll do it myself" type of games. What Project Wingman was for the Ace Combat community, Bloodstained is for all the starving Castlevania fans. Does it succeed? Hell if i know. I just wanted to try out one of those Metroidvania games and this one here was on Discount for just one and a half bucks.  Anyway, i´ve plaed it for 10 hours at this point and while it didn´t blew me out of the water, somehow i kept coming back to this game which means i kinda like it so far. It feels like a PS2 game with all its menus, the dialogue boxes, the item crafting and its sound effects as well as the clunky animations. All of this seems to be deliberate, tough as i felt reminded of an older generation of games where not every game had to be a Sony 3rd person action game where people won´t shut up for hours.  Gameplay reminded me a bit of the adventure mode from Smash Bros. Brawl out of all games. The game has an "open world", which is basically a 2d Labyrinth with several stages where you need specific skills to progress. For example, you can only explore the cistern once you acquired a skill that lets you propel yourself forward under water. Combat is simple, you swing your weapon, you hit and if you get hit or touch an enemy, that means damage. Not a fan of the latter, tough. I can understand that i take damage from a fire ghost but an old man brushing me shouldn´t deplete my hit points as much as it did. Maybe psychic damage from disguist? Whatever. You are not agile enough in this game for this to be a problem, leading to many situations where i was one pixel off and had to waste a potion because the sceen got so cluttered with enemies.  Also, you have different weapons in this game. Sword do the most reliable damage and i went well with those, but their hitbox is microscopic. There are enemies you can´t even hit because they are only waist high and you slash your sword above waist. Hell, even crouching didn´t help sometimes.  Also, some of the bosses are just nonsene. There´s the afforementioned old man. He fights by spamming debuff potions and runs away. Yeah, any bossfight that reminds me of Micolash from Bloodborne is a bad bossfight. Then there´s your clone who feels like she was designed for a different type of game entirely. Some of her attacks were just straight out unavoidable and since you´re relatively slow in this game and your clone moves like a super sayajin, you´ll get slammed a few times. I just don´t understand the design here. The first boss is so absurdly powerful that you will think you were too far already when it´s actually the correct boss you need to fight. Then later bosses are a cakewalk. There´s a two headed dragon who climbs a tower and tries to rip you apart from both sides of the screen. Was it hard? Nope. First try, maybe one or two potions had to be used but the rest was easy.  Then you get your face rearranged by the old man. Okay.  And why is there no real dodge? You can press a button to jump backwards, but since this is rather slow and directional bound, it´s practically useless in more hectic situations. Why not a dodge roll where you can control the direction? Would have made more sense and then i would have used it more often.  Oh and i would really appreciate if there was some sort of saving system outside of safe rooms. Fighting through several corridors of monsters and a lava pit is not very motivating when there´s still no safe room insight and a dragon blocks my path. If you die without a safe, all progress will be lost. No autosave, no quicksave, nothing. Sorry, Bloodstained, but my countries Prime Minister said my generation needs to work 80h a week. Can´t afford a setback like that anymore. Even the Castlevania retro games respect your time properly.  Anyway, i am nagging quite a bit, aren´t i? That´s because all in all, i actually like this game a lot. I mean, i gave it a thumbs up because it is geniuenely a good platformer with combat, exploration, good music and very good character design. It´s obviously a Castlevania clone but still manages to keep its own identity.  And i can totally understand why people are into these kinds of games now.

메트로베니아 장르 원조맛집에서 만든 게임이라서 파밍하면서 성장하는 재미가 있다. 근데 확실한 장점만큼이나 호불호 요소가 있는 게임이라서 참고하고 사면 될꺼 같다.( 약간의 스포 포함)   1. 19년도 게임 그래픽이라고 생각되지 않는다.   처음봤을때, KOF 14 보는줄 알았다. 그래도 계속 보다보면 눈썩는다는 생각보다는 어느정도 적응이 되긴하는편.   2. 노가다 요소가 생각보다 많다.   음식, 무기, 갑옷, 포션 만드는데. 전부 하나 같이 일단 한번은 만들어야한다. 스킬 강화 요소도 특정 쟤료템을 얻어야 하다보니 노가다가 강제되는 부분이 있다.  그 놈의 우유는 더럽게 나오지도 않으면서 요구량은 겁나 많아서 이거만 하루종일 캔듯.  물론 나는 스펙따윈 필요없고 컨으로 깨겠다는 사람은 크게 할 필요 없음.   3. 키보드 조작이 생각보다 거지같다.  대표적인 예시로 키다운 스킬이 E에 있다. 방향키는 WASD이다보니 키다운하고 있으면 손가락 꼬여 죽을맛이다.  큇슬록도 쓰는게 너무 불편해서 쓰지도 않았다.여러가지로 게임패드 생각하고 만든게임같다.   4. 오의 스킬 커맨드가 이걸 실전에서 쓰라고 만든건지 모르겠다.   성능도 병신인데, 커맨드도 거지같은 오의가 생각보다 많다. 옛날 캐슬베니아때 시스템들고 온건데. 굳이 들고와서 사람 불편하게 만들 필요가 있을까 싶음.  5. 후반으로 갈수록 게임이 허전하다.   이게 초중반에는 몸비틀면서 게임하던게 후반에 가면 몹재탕밖에 안나온다. 거기다가 최종보스도 딸깍하면 죽어버리고  마감에 쫒긴건지 모르겠는데. 후반가면 힘이 많이 빠지는 느낌이다.   마지막으로 음성은 일어로 듣길 추천한다. 영어로 하면 신음소리 장난아님.

As of this review, I am still in my first playthrough with 68% of the map explored. Bloodstained is an absolute blast to play. You can truly build how you want to build with tons of spells, passives, special armor, accessories, etc. This game reminds me of the old-school, side-scroll dungeon-crawlers. You have to remember what you're told in the main story since there isn't a method to see where you need to specifically go. Just go explore and you'll find your way there eventually. Hidden walls, passageways, and chests galore. You do have a handy quest archive for the side quests though (thank goodness, as they do stack up). The gameplay is super smooth, making for a streamlined experience.   My only gripe is with the technique combos. You have to prepare for a quick string of directional inputs followed by the attack button, diagonal inputs included. I'm okay with up, down, left, right, but I've never been good at combos with diagonals. I'm sure there are people out there that enjoy them though. Oh, and turn off framerate smoothing. Makes the game stutter for some reason. Everything runs at cinematic quality just fine without it.

My impressions have settled on Bloodstained being just an alright game that captures the feel of old-school metroidvanias.   The 2.5D style looks nice with some memorable backdrops, though the aesthetic isnt cohesive, notably in enemy design. The writing is forgettable but holds some charm.   Levels are interconnected very well with lots of unique enemies, though movement even into endgame feels sluggish, and platforming just exists to connect areas.   There's lots of weapons and spells (shards) I had fun trying out. I appreciate how easy it was to swap between them, and most were fun to use. Certain abilities required to progress share shard slots, forcing you to constantly alternate.   Taking damage stuns you with short i-frames, often leading to a snowball of hit tanks. It wouldn't feel as bad if hitboxes werent wildly inconsistent.  I would recommend other games in the genre first, but this still managed to scratch the itch left behind.   Rating: 6.5 / 10

Maybe the game gets better somehow, but I really can't see how. The goofy fanservice character moves so slowly that the controls become poorly responsive, the hit detection is terrible all around, you take full damage every second your character is in range of an enemy's hit box, there is no dodge/dash (except for an awkward, slow hop backwards--only backwards), the dialog is atrocious (but not bad enough to become funny--just bad), the story is dull, there's a remarkable lack of customization?? (the weapons/armor system is overdone and yet somehow seems superfluous and I'm not sure if stats or leveling actually matter or do anything worthwhile?), and the gameplay is tedious and grindy. I suppose to the game's credit, the designers telegraphed that the plot/characters were terrible and gameplay was supposed to be tedious and grindy from the opening and it's my fault for not believing them, but I thought the game might improve once I started getting some of the skills.  tl;dr: if you play the intro and aren't madly in love with the slow clunky gameplay and godawful everything else, drop this awful game rather than trying to push through. It doesn't get better.

O que eu posso dizer desse jogo é que ele é excelente, um dos melhores do gênero metroidvania com certeza.  Pontos positivos: - Pra mim o ponto mais forte do jogo é a variedade imensa de armas e os fragmentos, tem MUITA coisa, e o jogo desperta aquele sentimento em você parecido com pokémon, aquela vontade de pegar todos. - Level design maneiro, como todo metroidvania é bem divertido explorar e você sente vontade de explorar tudo - Variedade de inimigos bem alta, embora tenha alguns que são reeskin, tem jogo triple A que não tem essa variedade - Direção de arte maneira  Pontos negativos: - Gráficos meio simples demais - Faltou capricho na parte das animações das armas, toda arma basicamente tem só uma animação de ataque, Dead Cells por exemplo é bem mais caprichado nessa parte, aonde cada arma tem sua animação própria e sequência de ataques diferentes - Alguns trechos finais parecem um pouco preguiçosos - História e personagens desinteressantes  Recomendo demais pra qualquer um que goste de metroidvania.

Gioco interessante, ma con evidenti problematiche forse dovute al budget limitato per la sua produzione; i contenuti sono moltissimi, le armi e le abilità numerosissime ma difficilmente sfruttabili appieno, le meccaniche dietro la grafica accattivante sono rudiementali, se impugnate una spada potrete attaccare solo in due direzioni, destra e sinistra, se impugnate uno spadone, il suo attacco disegnerà una mezzaluna sopra la vostra testa, ma non c'è modo di attaccare verso il basso, o in diagonale, se non con attacchi magici specifici, e non è possibile passare rapidamente da un'arma a un'altra se non passando per il menù principale o per una scorciatoia disponibile solo acquisendo la relativa abilità, ma che comunque aprirà un sotto menù; la maggior parte dei boss si possono fare fuori attaccando a testa bassa con l'incantesimo più potente disponibile, consumando qualche pozione o qualche alimento che ripristina energia e mana, e alcuni, temibili se affrontati in modo standard, diventano quasi inermi ultiizzando l'abilità di invertire la gravità, come se i loro pattern di attacco fossero stati studiati dagli sviluppatori quando questa caratteristica non era stata inserita nel gioco (forse è stata inserita dopo?). Ci sono numerosi finali ma ci si imbatte nel peggiore con estrema facilità, mentre per gli altri bisognerà sudare un pò di più, anche perchè i suggerimenti degli npc non vengono ripetuti. Le missioni secondarie sono date da npc che le snocciolano una dietro l'atra senza quasi alcuna variazione nei dialoghi, risultando sostanzialmente dei distributori di obiettivi che consistono tutti nell'uccidere un certo numero di una tipologia di mostro, E' un prodotto fatto con passione che divertirà certamente gli amanti del genere, ma non può reggere il confronto con altri esponenti più raffinati e meglio bilanciati.

Recommended....but on sale. With due respect to Iga, SOTN was a long time ago, and Bloodstained shows he's taken zero cues from all the similarly inspired games that have come since. This plays exactly like a HD version of the handheld Igavanias, which is what he pitched and got funded, but still - I expected more than a mediocre take on those games. The bosses are straight mediocre or bad, the symphonic rock soundtrack is predictable and mid, and the cell shaded 3D look is ugly as hell - they should have saved money on the soundtrack and spent the savings on actual, proper pixel art.   If you're coming to this from the likes of Hollow Knight, manage your expectations. This plays like a game from the mid to late nineties. I respect the deference to retro design, but the genre has moved on. And I'm saying this as someone who played and loved SOTN when it was released. I get they were working to a budget, but this just feels like less than it could have been. Still, Bloodstained isn't a disaster like Mighty Number 9 was, so it could have been far worse. As it stands, this is just ... okay. Inoffensive. Decent. Iga is better than that, or at least he was. The genre has moved on since the nineties, it appears Koji Igarashi has not.

这是一款让我思考良久后，将评价从差评改成好评的游戏。实在是难绷。 恶魔城的正统续作，但我不是粉丝，也没玩过曾经的作品。一切感受都是纯主观。 先说差评的原因： 首先，BOSS设计得很无趣。感觉就是设计师纯粹的梦到什么就设计什么。 然后是美术。非常不像19年的游戏，像09年的。 接着是实在无聊的长时间刷宝。现在已经不是80年代了，用刷宝拖长单价游戏的时间根本就是欠差评。 最后是挺无聊的剧情的。 基于上述原因，我一开始给了差评。 后来我理解了这个游戏的真正玩法——刷爆之后无双。 什么BOSS设计？什么拉胯的美术？什么乱七八糟的玩意儿？一身神装+各种流氓招式朝着任何堵你路的家伙脸上招呼过去就行了。

[b]Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night[/b] is a textbook example on how a modern metroidvania game should be made. This game has everything going for it - solid storyline, good and versatile gameplay loop, many different enemies, decently sized map that's not too small nor gigantic, and a main character, Miriam, that you actually care about. I thoroughly enjoyed playing this game even though it has a good amount of backtracking if you are looking to fully experience what it has to offer. What I honestly liked the most is how detailed your character customization is, you can fully edit Miriam's appearance and switch separate pieces of equipment that allow you to fully tailor your style of play. This game introduces a concept of special abilities, or shards as they are called here, which let you select the ones you like to play with or are mostly suitable to you, and this is a standout mechanic.  On the downside, the game can feel a bit obnoxious with it's saving system since save rooms are scattered all around the map and there's no autosave, so consider saving very, very often. Trust me, you don't want to die after playing for 30-40 minutes just to get back to the starting point. That's infuriating and it happened few times to me.  All in all, there's a lot of love poured into creating this game, and it definitely shows. It's an absolute gem and I'm very happy I got to play it.  9/10

You level, you get abilities from killing monsters that are called shards and there is a ton of weapons and gear options.  Some of the things to figure out are downright fun if you don't use a guide.  The blood ability was obvious but on my first playthrough on playstation some years ago I quit for a while because I couldn't figure out how to proceed at a certain point and killing the final boss gave a bad ending.  Anyway I tried again and realize it was one of those abilities that I needed to use in the water to get to the next boss fight and exploration area,  It was rewarding figuring that out.  Anyway, good fun for me.  The techniques for each weapon seem to be similar movements as games like Street Fighter 2 and Mortal Combat.  Some are really great like the Kung Fu shoes technique. Once you master it you can use them with any weapon of the same category so the ability can be used on more powerful versions.  I also like the spear charge technique but you may find others you dig.  Characters look good and its voice acted well unlike most modern games so thats a big plus.

As a Castlevania fan who's played most classicvanias and Metroidvania's in the series:  Impression at First few hour: Loving it so far - This game uses: 1) Souls mechanics from the Sorrow games 2) Difficulty and tight boss mechanics from Order of Ecclesia  Impression at later hours: This game is WAY too grindy. The crafting/cooking system for upgrading powers/stats is way too over-built and tedious. The people who really love this game seem to be people who love farming/grinding (maybe that's your cup of tea, but it's not for me personally)  Besides wasting your time, the upgrade system is also completely unbalanced. Fights go from feeling unfair to completely trivial after a few upgrades (do you like your steak burned to a crisp or bloody as hell?)  Dark Souls is a great example of a balanced progression system, where upgrades become slowly available and give you an "edge" in combat without making the game too easy.

I had this game with a few hours on it sitting in my steam library for years until finally forcing myself to give it more of a shot and came out thoroughly loving it. Miriam embodying a sort of stained glass theme in her design is so extremely cool and fresh it can't be understated.   The artstyle initially felt a bit odd to me and even after playing 50 hours a lot of it still is but you get used to it, kickstarter paintings and the giant cat and dog heads sticking out like a sore thumb imo. About 60% of the shards and weapons in this game feel sorta useless as by the time you obtain them they kinda suck and was thoroughly disappointed after crafting the weapon on the cover for the game just for it to be pretty underwhelming outside of its weapon arte.   I very much enjoyed the 100% for this game as i love farming and there was a lot of it. I'm glad I was wrong about this one because seeing the sequel inspired me to go back and give it another shot. For all of its oddities it ends up adding quite a lot of character to the game that im sure to remember.

[b]Bloodstained[/b] è il successore spirituale della serie [b]Castlevania[/b], opera dello suo storico creatore [i]Koji Igarashi[/i] dopo l’addio a [b]Konami[/b]. Partito in [i]crowdfunding[/i] nel 2015, il titolo si presenta come un [i]Metroidvania[/i] con grafica 2.5D e riprende in modo evidente le meccaniche da [b]Symphony of the Night[/b] (1997). Siamo davanti ad un classico del genere, dove l’esplorazione rappresenta il vero punto di forza. La mappa è vasta e ricca di percorsi alternativi; nemici e [i]Boss[/i] sono ben distribuiti, rendendo la progressione sempre coinvolgente. Il [i]combat system[/i] è davvero vario e soddisfacente, grazie alle diverse tipologie di armi principali e secondarie. Non mancano abilità e oggetti da sbloccare, spesso essenziali per proseguire o accedere a zone nascoste. Le principali criticità riguardano la veste grafica: modelli e animazioni 3D risultano talvolta poco rifiniti, mentre alcune [i]hitbox[/i] poco curate non rendono giustizia al [i]gameplay[/i]. La [i]main quest[/i] si completa in circa 15 ore, ma tra [i]sidequest[/i], aree opzionali, modalità [i]extra[/i] e una grande quantità di equipaggiamento da sperimentare, la longevità può facilmente superare le 30-40 ore.  [b]PRO:[/b] - [i]level design[/i] stimolante, ricco di segreti - numerose meccaniche e abilità da provare - enorme varietà di armi, nemici e [i]Boss[/i] - colonna sonora eccelsa, come da tradizione  [b]CONTRO:[/b] - modelli poligonali e animazioni abbastanza grezze - alcune [i]hitbox[/i] imprecise e da rivedere  [b]VOTO: 8[/b]   Nonostante gli anni sulle spalle, resta un ottimo esponente del genere, ma non riesce a rinnovarsi abbastanza rispetto alla concorrenza.

I followed the development of this a bit all the way back from the Kickstarter, but then put off getting it for years and then put off playing it for months. I've played through about a dozen metroidvania games, so I knew what to expect going in. It's definitely a spiritual successor/spin-off to Castlevania. Symphony of the Night (1997) redefined the castlevania franchise and is the foundation of the metroidvania genre, and it falls in line.  Bloodstained reminds me a lot of Order of Ecclesia (2009) in terms of characters/npcs, side quests, the hub, etc. The shard system is also very similar to the soul system from Aria of Sorrow (2003).  The graphics are pretty good. I always gravitate towards 2d > 3d with sidescrolling classics, but this one does a solid job of keeping the visuals serviceably clear and playable while having nice detail to enjoy too.  The voice acting is wonderful. The sfx are extremely similar to Castlevania. They're about what you'd expect. I feel like the soundtrack is kind of flat though. Not terrible, but nothing amazing either. Was expecting better from that.  Think I'm about 1/4th done with the game now. Pacing and direction is pretty simple. Just keep going where you can go and filling out the map. Haven't needed to take notes, look anything up, or even pause to remember something. Only fight that's been a bit tough so far was Zangetsu. Was being impatient and not wanting to dodge, but he had quite a bit of HP for so early on. Leveling up is curved back a bit. Feels like you don't level up much, the level up messages you see are for familiars.  Edit: About 75% done now. At a loss as to what to do next. Not sure if I want to use guides, compare maps or just put it on pause for now. Got the bad ending at like 50% completion. Hoped around slow and steady. Got to about 70% and came to a crawl to find anything else that I can do. Just did the 8bit boss and it was a dead end. NPCs giving out no guidance now.  Last update: endgame is  crazy grindy. Never  would've found the last like 5% of hidden items and randomness without looking it up. Wiki for enemy drops, money farming guide, etc really carried me through the last bit. Bout ready for new game+ or playing through as different characters. Or I might hit the other Bloodstained games first and  come back. But lots of replayability and end/post game content for those that are into it.

[h1] [b] Духовный наследник серии игр Castlevania [/b] [/h1]  [b] Сюжет разворачивается в Англии XVIII века. Алхимики призвали демонов, используя детей с кристаллами (оскольщиков). Главная героиня Мириам, заснувшая на 10 лет, пробуждается и пытается остановить своего друга Джибеля, который призвал адских созданий, чтобы отомстить человечеству, параллельно борясь с проклятием, пожирающим его тело [/b]  [h2] [b] Плюсы: [/b] [/h2]  [b] 1) Отличный саундтрек, не прибавить не убавить [/b]  [b] 2) Красивые визуальные эффекты магии, умений и т д [/b]  [b] 3) Великолепная японская озвучка, при участии таких легендарных актеров как Ами Кошимидзу [/b]  [b] 4) Разнообразие боссов, все они уникальные и запоминающиеся со своими механиками. После победы над ними больше вы их в игре не встретите, что хорошо, ибо зачастую в некоторых играх боссы могут потом стать рядовыми элитными врагами [/b]  [b] 5) Приятный разнообразный геймплей. Игра довольно часто выдает вам новую магию или же оружие, поэтому попутно проходя игру вы можете экспериментировать с этим, оружия и магии в игре очень большое количество. Различные оружия имеют уникальные техники которые можно выучить применив их какое то количество раз, после этого техники переносятся на весь вид этого оружия. Например вы выучили технику одноручного меча, и теперь эта техника переносится на все одноручные мечи, которые вы будете находить, крафтить и т д [/b]  [b] 6) Кастомизация внешности героини. Можно менять прически, одежду, аксессуары лица, головы и т д. Мелочь но приятное дополнение [/b]  [b] 7) Большое количество отсылок к играм Castlevania. Фанатам точно понравится [/b]  [h2] [b] Минусы: [/b] [/h2]  [b] 1) Сомнительные баланс. Я долго думал об этом пункте и все же решил его отнести к недостаткам. Магии и оружия в игре много, но невооруженным глазом можно сразу понять глядя на магию или оружие что оно либо ситуативное (в лучшем случае) либо бесполезное. Два примера - магия отравления полезна только на различных боссов у которых нет резиста, в обычных ситуациях/забегах вы вряд ли будете ее использовать, оружие копья, которые не могут бить под углом, что очень важно в этом игровом жанре, при этом в игре есть оружие кнут, которое покрывает всю область перед героиней во время удара, что просто мотивирует вас использовать именно его так как оно попросту универсальное [/b]  [b] 2) Большое количество гринда для прохождения игры на 100%. Вам придется заниматься рутиной, а именно для примера вам нужно победить всех врагов в игре хотя бы по разу, собрать с них все доступные предметы и осколки. Не скажу что это веселое занятие, а главное отнимает огромное количество времени, за которое вы можете пройти игру два раза. Если не упарываться с прокачку, то боссы могут показаться довольно тяжелым испытанием так как способов лечения в игре довольно мало, в основном это зелья, которые тут стоят довольно дорого, можно конечно их крафтить но на это тоже уходит время. Кстати про крафт, для 100% вам нужно купить и скрафтить все предметы в игре которые только есть. Благо игру можно комфортно пройти без всего выше перечисленного, так что этот минус скорей натянутый если вы хотите 100% достижений [/b]  [b] 3) Местами в игре проседает fps без каких либо причин, не сказать что это сильно мешает, но на глаз заметно и иногда может раздражать [/b]  [b] 4) Метки на картах иногда багуются на мини карте во время геймплея [/b]   [h2] [b] Итог: [/b] [/h2]  [b] Не плохая метроидвания, мне понравилось. Учитывая все плюсы и минусы ставлю этой игре 7-8 баллов из 10 [/b]

说来惭愧，明明是类银恶魔城爱好者，银河战士也玩了究极和生存恐惧，恶魔城却一直没认真打过——血污之前断断续续玩了2-3次，月下也找了模拟器玩了一阵，但奈何就是玩不进去，听说血污续作要上了，终于还是靠着sd把血污打了一轮。  先说缺点吧，这也是为什么之前玩了几次都没玩完的原因，就是恶魔城的精髓是跑图探索，动作部分相比现代类银恶魔城其实弱很多，别的不说，满屏幕乱飞的蛤蟆和x元素就没少让我破防，加上搓招系统玩不明白，一开始用动作类银恶魔城的思路玩确实有点受罪。  但，等刷到后面，一身装备技能组合着玩的时候，这个游戏的爽感就开始了，你会发现大部分boss真的是可以靠吃药站撸硬怼过去的，敌人的出招记不清楚？不需要，装备够狠就碾过去了，有了加速器大跳和真空刃你就变成推土机了，真正的上天入地没你去不了的地方，总体来说除了前期对动作类玩家有点坐牢，中后期其实没什么硬卡点。  最后，这款作品对我来说其实是个7--8之间的得分，给一般有点低，但你说他能到8分的优秀客观来说也有差距，主要是剧情和地图设计对比今天的后辈们很难说特别出彩，但作为恶魔城本城的现代版本，对类型爱好者们而言还是值得去欣赏和体验一番的，不然其他几款作品的设计交互实在是太老了，血污至少能让你更好的理解恶魔城在这个类型里贡献的设计要素是什么，也更能理解为什么银河恶魔城共同构成了这个游戏门类。

Meu amigo, que jogo bom do caralho. Mal comparando, mas lembra muito Dark Souls, pela temática medieval, variedade de builds, level design, finais alternativos, etc. Sem contar que ainda colocaram uma protagonista gostosa hehe.   Acho que a única coisa que não gostei muito é a necessidade constante de craftar... Nisso eu acho que o jogo foi para os caralho kkkkk. Praticamente tudo que vc queira fazer no jogo, você precisa craftar. Seja set, armas, itens consumíveis, magias, upgrades, etc Isso é muito chato...  Então basicamente se você de inicio não jogar com uma build focada no atributo sorte, vai ser muito difícil e demorado para conseguir obter os itens necessários, principalmente os de upgrade. No mais o jogo é muito bom e vale a pena demais jogar e ter a experiência. Super recomendo!

dawn of sorrow if the main character wasn't annoying, for better and for worse, since i don't like either of the sorrow games.   this feels like if dawn of sorrow had more time to cook, basically. it has aspects of other igavania games mixed in too, the motion input moves make this by far the most enjoyable combat that iga's ever made, i always liked those in sotn but werent used very often so you'd often forget about them, they were also relatively niche and specific and with sotn being a cakewalk of a game there wasn't any need for them either unless you wanted to so getting to use them and actually feeling like i kinda need them to fight is a breath of fresh air, i like that this game is harder than most of iga's other work too, around the same difficulty of order of ecclesia i'd say but with less focus on damage types for the most part if you get the 'weapon techniques' down.  shards are literally just souls, but with somehow less variety than the ones in the sorrow games. 20% actually usable stuff 70% useless garbage. crafting system is fine, less boring than dawn, the questing system that ecclesia had is here and i think less interesting since part of why i enjoyed quests in ecclesia was for the tooltips and subtle character development shanoa would get throughout, as well as the interactions between her and the npc too. you don't get it that much here, especially since miriam can just be a person with emotions and memories here.  storywise this is kinda just the same quality as the sorrow duology (so bad) none of the characters are particularily compelling, the story is very much shounen jump and there's a subtle disdain for women throughout it just like every other igavania from sotn to ooe  there's always interesting things you can extrapolate thematically but it's kinda just filler textually.  if you like the gameplay, movement and tech of igavanias with a bigger focus on sotn-style motion inputs for combat this is a really fun game and you'll feel right at home, this koji igarashi guy knows how to make a good metroidvania, its like his one skill. in my arbitrary tier list of igavania games this is 3rd place above SotN but below PoR and OoE imo, it's just a bit too long and starts to slog towards the end along with my typical complaints about his games' writing.   i'm posting this while im near the end of the game so maybe my opinion will change when i finish the game proper but i highly doubt it since i've played these games before. edit: yea, no changes in my opinion.

Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night (далее B:RotN) - для меня оказалась одной из тех игр, о которой хорошее впечатление появляется сразу. Атмосфера данной игры напоминает игры 2000-2010, по своему простой и в то же время сложный геймплей, к тому же, он действительно оригинальный - вид сбоку! Напоминает игры в жанре рогалик (к которым у меня был слабый интерес), но эта игра затмила все мои ожидания, чему я действительно рада. Приятная, не надоедающая музыка, соответствует локациям. Также в B:RotN имеется автоматическая система уровней; есть возможность кастомизировать героя, тем самым придавая ему не только приятный образ на ваш вкус, но и множество преимуществ в бою (усиление силы атаки, защиты и т.п.). Я ни капли не жалею, что приобрела эту игру, поэтому могу посоветовать ее!  P.S.  стоит отметить, что в данную игру и жанр я играю впервые.

Honestly, just over-long, bloated and a waste of time. Way too many different things at once. The design amounts to the gameplay loop being like: grind for one item, to get another item, to craft together with another grinding item to fuse to make a weapon you break down to get another resource that you then need to merge with another ingredient and do this 12 times in a row, all for a 1hp increase and maybe progress another 1% through the map. It's just constant roadblocks and annoyance, and SO. MUCH. GRINDING.   Also about fourteen different systems too many. Crafting, cooking, weaponsmithing, shardmaking, you name it. VERY bloated. Just makes for an overall opaque and irritating experience. It's all the WORST and most bloated design aspects of a JRPG glued onto a mid platformer.

Foi o jogo que me fez realmente me apaixonar pelo gênero metroidvania.  Eu comecei sem muita expectativa, mas rapidamente virei aquele jogador que quer explorar cada canto do mapa. O jogo te incentiva muito a isso, sempre tem uma sala escondida, um caminho que você ainda não consegue acessar ou algum segredo que fica na cabeça até você voltar mais forte. Essa sensação de ir abrindo o mapa aos poucos é viciante.  O sistema de habilidades foi o que mais me prendeu. Cada inimigo pode te dar uma habilidade diferente, então você fica o tempo todo testando combinações novas. Isso deixa o combate bem dinâmico. Dá pra adaptar bastante ao seu estilo, o que eu curto muito.  A progressão também funciona muito bem. Você sente claramente quando fica mais forte, seja por habilidade nova, equipamento ou até pelas pequenas melhorias que vai acumulando. E o jogo recompensa quem gosta de se aprofundar.  Visualmente eu gosto bastante da ambientação. O castelo tem aquele estilo clássico, meio sombrio, mas ao mesmo tempo bonito. E a trilha sonora ajuda muito a entrar no clima, principalmente nas lutas contra chefes.  No geral, foi o jogo que me fez entender de verdade o apelo do gênero. Não é só sobre avançar, é sobre voltar, explorar, testar coisas novas e ir dominando o mapa aos poucos. Foi uma experiência que me prendeu por muitas horas justamente por isso.

[h1][b]🎮 Reseña de Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night[/b][/h1]  [h2]Resumen del Juego[/h2] Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night es el “no-no-es-Castlevania-pero-sí-es-Castlevania” en versión metroidvania 2.5D, con acción lateral, exploración obsesiva y un toque de rol que te hace abrir menús como si fueran cofres. Controlas a Miriam, protagonista femenina con estética anime gótica, atrapada en un castillo que claramente no pasó inspección de seguridad: monstruos, trampas, secretos y una cantidad de botín que te hace sentir que estás saqueando un catálogo de cosplay oscuro.  La propuesta es simple y peligrosamente efectiva: explora, desbloquea movilidad, farmea habilidades, fabrica cosas, sube estadísticas y repite hasta que el mapa deje de tener “neblina” y tu vida social también. La primera impresión es buenísima: se siente como una carta de amor al género… escrita con pluma elegante, sí, pero con manchas de “vamos a meter de todo” por todas partes.  Y sí: tiene un montón de opciones modernas (subtítulos, dificultad ajustable, jugable sin eventos rápidos, Steam Cloud, Remote Play, mando), lo cual es irónico porque el juego te trata como si tuvieras 200 horas libres y la paciencia de un monje.  [h2]Lo Sagrado y Lo Maldito[/h2]  Lo Bueno: ✅ Exploración metroidvania adictiva 🗺️: el castillo te engancha con rutas, llaves de movilidad y secretos bien escondidos.   ✅ Sistema de rol y progresión “solo una misión más” 📈: niveles, equipo y habilidades que premian la obsesión.   ✅ Fabricación y personalización 🔧: para quienes disfrutan convertir ingredientes raros en poder (o en excusas para farmear).   ✅ Banda sonora con vibra gótica potente 🎼: acompaña perfecto el tono de fantasía oscura.   ✅ Opciones de accesibilidad y comodidad 🎮: mando, subtítulos, dificultad ajustable, sin QTE… gracias por no castigar mis reflejos de adulto.  Lo No Tan Bueno: ❌ Exceso de “contenido picante” 🙄: desnudos/sexualización que se siente más como guiño barato que como aporte real a la atmósfera.   ❌ 2.5D que a veces se ve raro 📺: hay momentos donde lo “anime gótico” luce increíble y otros donde luce… “indie con presupuesto peleado”.   ❌ Multijugador/JcJ en la ficha, pero el alma del juego es solitaria 🤷: vienes por un jugador; lo demás se siente más como checklist que como identidad.   ❌ Curva de farmeo y crafting que puede volverse rutina ⏳: si no te gusta repetir para optimizar, el castillo también tiene salidas… (mentira, no las tiene).    [h2]Tabla de Evaluación[/h2] [table] [tr][th]Aspecto[/th][th]Puntuación (0-10)[/th][th]Comentario[/th][/tr] [tr][td]Jugabilidad[/td][td]9[/td][td]Metroidvania sólido: moverte y pelear se siente delicioso.[/td][/tr] [tr][td]Dificultad / IA[/td][td]8[/td][td]Ajustable y retadora; a veces más por diseño que por “genialidad” enemiga.[/td][/tr] [tr][td]Contenido[/td][td]9[/td][td]Exploración, progreso, equipo, fabricación… hay castillo para rato.[/td][/tr] [tr][td]Gráficos / Video[/td][td]7[/td][td]Estilo atractivo, pero el 2.5D no siempre envejece con dignidad.[/td][/tr] [tr][td]Historia[/td][td]7[/td][td]Cumple: gótico, oscuro, melodramático… lo justo para seguir matando cosas.[/td][/tr] [tr][td]Ambientación[/td][td]9[/td][td]Castillo gótico con identidad; te dan ganas de perderte (y de quejarte).[/td][/tr] [tr][td]Efectos (VFX)[/td][td]8[/td][td]Habilidades y golpes se ven satisfactorios sin volverse puro ruido visual.[/td][/tr] [tr][td]Música[/td][td]9[/td][td]Gran banda sonora: épica, oscura y memorable.[/td][/tr] [tr][td]Voces[/td][td]7[/td][td]Correctas: no arruinan, no elevan a leyenda.[/td][/tr] [tr][td]Diversidad[/td][td]6[/td][td]Variedad de enemigos/zonas bien; en representación, bastante estándar.[/td][/tr] [tr][td]Rendimiento[/td][td]7[/td][td]Generalmente estable, pero no es el “benchmark” de optimización.[/td][/tr] [tr][td]Rejugabilidad[/td][td]8[/td][td]Builds, farmeo y completismo: el juego te invita a volver.[/td][/tr] [tr][td][b]Total Puntuaciones (Excelente)[/b][/td][td][b]7.9[/b][/td][td]Un metroidvania que casi roza lo top, con detalles discutibles y mucho encanto.[/td][/tr] [/table]  [h2]Conclusión (Excelente)[/h2] Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night es lo que pasa cuando alguien entiende el metroidvania, lo respeta, y además decide que tú no mereces una vida fuera del mapa. Es divertido, profundo y muy satisfactorio si te gusta explorar, mejorar tu personaje y convertir cada rincón del castillo en una lista de pendientes.  Pero también es el típico juego que a ratos se cree “edgy” por meter contenido sexual/visual provocador, y eso puede chocar: no arruina la experiencia, pero sí se siente como ese amigo que cuenta el mismo chiste incómodo en la reunión familiar. I've yet to 100% all collectibles but I'm leaving that for a rainy day, leaving this game satisfied by completing it as much as I could.  A huge thank you to developers for making this. I delayed playing it for a long time and I'm glad I played it when I did.

If you're wondering how this compares to old Castlevania games, despite the many references to Symphony of the Night, this is Aria/Dawn of Sorrow. The music and 'shard' variety are probably the strongest aspects of the game.  I'm a big, big Castlevania fan. But the game just isn't a successor series. The 3D is awful, the weapon hitboxes are at times complete jank. The difficulty spikes immediately for the second boss then the game gets easier and easier, culminating with the acquisition of the upside-down power which breaks almost every boss fight in the game. Too many quests, the second to last area in the game is the worst idea for an area I've ever seen, and the game itself overstays its welcome.  I've accepted that Castlevania is dead and that's okay.

A game that feels like whoever was making it was scared there would never be another Metroidvania after it. It has every hallmark of the genre and plays like a best hits version of the previous Metroidvania games mechanics.  In terms of style, music and direction, it's really something special. While it still hangs out in the gothic clubhouse, it has enough distinct charm to make it stand on it's own feet. Although when it comes to cutscenes, things do get a bit stiff on the animation department.   However, in terns of gameplay I find myself torn. This is a huge map and there's so many little things to do within it. Although that is sometimes to a fault as when you start out with lacking abilities, certain missteps can cause back tracking for days. Even when backtracking for story sake, it can take quite awhile to get around.  More on the combat side, I'm not the keenest on the crystal shard system (aka the soul system from aria of sorrow). I respect it big time as a mechanic, as the thrill of every enemy having a new move for you to use is a lot of fun. Furthermore, this game better enables you to stick with the moves you like so you can upgrade any ability you fancy. However, I get bothered with how difficult it is to make decent move sets with them. The game gives you a shortcut option where you can swap between gear and crystals and save pre configs. But it's too much being adjusted in one go. Using older Castlevania games as reference, it was efficient and promoted experimentation when swaps were quick and just changed your moves. But in this, you have to update all your armour and weapons with it and by the time you find a new piece of gear your config is out of date and you have to rebuild the bunch. It just put me off from trying out moves by the latter half as I didn't want to deal with it, keeping it just for mandatory traversal moves.  In terms of challenge and difficulty, this had some great intensity to it but I swear this game has a few jank hitboxes. The greatest move some of these bosses in this game can pull on you is walking into you and not moving. With the high damage numbers and the low i-frames after hit, it's a recipe for a quick game over. So many of the boss fights had to be played with less worries about avoiding moves and more avoiding the boss itself.  While my complaints are elaborate though, I still think this game is a great time. There's so much charm and heart poured into it. Furthermore, if you take to this, this game offers so much post game content that it would make your head spin. From extra characters, to boss rushes, to randomisers, speedrun modes and even entirely new classic modes too.

iim conflicted on this one. i dont hate it and i really liked the gameplay loop, i was very engaged with it. its just the game is full of outdated design choices from the old castelvania games! here is a list of those bad designs:  1) the saves are manual only and if you die while having a far away save file from where you died, you are gonna have to replay everything till that point!   2) each weapon has special moves associated with it but you can only excute them with those clunky quarter circle motions which are not intitive at all on my rog ally handheld and its just much easier to kill the enemy quickly with normal means.   3) some of the set pieces that are essential for game progression are difficult to decipher, for instance, there is a library location with moving book shelfs that you need to jump on to progress, but you have to point at them first!! the problem is that the indicator on those book shelfs is innocuous and can be easliy missed.  4) some of those boss fights are very easy to cheese with magic, i barely lost to any bosses.  at the end of it, the game is fun, the story is engaging, and i enjoyed my time with it. so it gets a 7/10 from me, buy it on discount!

Раз 3 уже наверное прохожу эту игру, и только сейчас наконец-то закончил(потому что склероз не дремлет и вспомнить че там было 5-6 лет назад - невозможно) Пройдя перед этим почти все Castlevania, к которым был причастен Ига(и не только) решил таки перепройти данную метроидванию. И че хочется сказать... Нормально В целом сюжет держится на стандартной кастваниевской формуле - прийти в замок - дать пизды демонам желательно не на плохую концовку(которой здесь по факту нет, но неважно). Но в этот раз, насобирав на кикстартере 5 лимонов дораров, Ига решил ебнуть все свои кастлвании в одну большую игру: тут вам и души демонов из aria\dawn of sorrow, крафт в целом оттуда же, всякие уникальные пушки со всех частей(типа криссагрима из SoTN и летающего меча), унылые квесты из order of ecclisia(К СОЖАЛЕНИЮ), ♥♥♥♥♥♥ молотоглавы, которые по-факту просто рескин бошек медуз с их окаменением(будь ты проклят, Ига) и многое, многое другое Про плюсы особо говорить нечего, ибо ждать чего-то хуевого от человека, который собаку съел на метроидваниях я не буду. Визуал, музыка(мне не особо запомнилась, но она хорошая), босс-файты, в целом трайверс по всему замку и его строение, сюжет, не душный в целом крафт и система осколков К минус хочется отнести эти квесты. Честно, я не знаю к чему их было делать и почему ЕДИНСТВЕННЫЙ УНИКАЛЬНЫЙ КВЕСТ - ЭТО СПАСТИ ПРИДУРКА КОТОРЫЙ НЕ МОЖЕТ ДО ХАБА ДОЙТИ 3 РАЗА. Все остальные квесты заключаются ровно в трёх вещах: бабке - принести пожрать, женщине у надгробий - шмотку на могилку и бабе в самом хабе - убей того-то того-то. Всё. И не то чтобы они прям сложные, они скорее дрочные, ибо награда за них частенько очень сомнительная. Проще просто ходить в шмотках с повышенной удачей и дропом лута и наковырять с какой-нибудь суккубши условный Звериный Хлыст, чем надеется от них получать какую-нибудь W шмотку. Так же хочется всё-таки привести к минусам то, что в игре ну... немного перебор с отсылками. Типа, да, фанбаза кастлы слюнями брызжет на то что библиотекарю(который отсылка сразу и на Алукарда, и на Графа Орлока, которого после аниме по кастле сделали персонажем, а не просто зеленой глистой из SoTN, и на Дракулу) можно с комнаты ниже влететь прыжком в жопу и тот выронит какую-то шмотку, но типа... Можно же было побольше всяких новых приколов сделать, а не срать отсылками. За пять мультов то. Ну и микро-минус чисто от меня - это очень поздно открытая возможность крафта местного Криссагрима и улучшеного летающего меча. Я хотел раньше В остальном - игра отличная, если вы никогда не мацали кастлвании и метроидвании в целом. Это вот самый классический вариант(и самых лучший ИМХО) среди всех. На уровне с симфонией он, конечно, не будет, но поиграть в неё более, чем стоит. Просто делайте квесты в полсилы и не фокусироваться на них. Ну и режимы за других персонажей тоже неплохие( кроме Авроры, сорри, но после героинового забега за Зангецу она ощущается как резидент слиперская шляпа, даже несмотря на уникальное повествование через стихи и немного отличающийся геймплей) Моя оценка - 8.5 зеточек рапирой из 10.

How to Resurrect a Genre without Selling Your Soul to a Pachinko Machine. Is Bloodstained the second coming of Alucard?  Frequently, and somewhat lazily, described as a successor to Symphony of the Night. In reality, the product functions as a high-definition distillation of the "Handheld Era" specifically the lineage of GBA and DS titles such as Aria of Sorrow and Order of Ecclesia where Igarashi and his team spent years refining the art of high asset efficiency and modular system design under the constraints of limited hardware. Those handheld titles were masterclasses in compact spatial reasoning, and Bloodstained attempts to port that specific density into the expansive, and often precarious, environment of the Unreal Engine 4. The transition to 2.5D was famously turbulent, requiring a comprehensive visual overhaul after early consumer feedback suggested the aesthetic was, to put it politely, serviceable but uninspired. (Okay, if you’ve read this far, thanks, and I’ll reward you by spilling the tea: people on the internet didn’t just call the game’s graphics “serviceable but uninspired.” They said it had… poop-graphics. Well, aren’t you here for reading about poop graphics?) The final result is a visual experience that oscillates between gothic grandeur and the uncanny valley of “Asset Store” aesthetics, creating a disjointed atmosphere where high-fidelity lighting often clashes with somewhat stiff character animations.  Whether it’s the overall design or the insany choices in certain cutscenes (which, while broken, are just sassy), Miriam looks like she’s ready for a “Gothic-Lolita-Industrial” photoshoot for Stained Glass Weekly. When Miriam’s character model plays her 57 scarf animations, you’ll ask yourself: is she just serving the physics engine… or is she also serving ♥♥♥♥? And I’m not only talking about the main characters. Scenes like the castle zoom after the intro stage, or the animations when shards are acquired, the player gains a level or discovers a new area, or just the save room or fast travel room… you’ll ask yourself: “Is this atmospheric depth, or am I ready for superficial betrayal and alchemy in a castle that makes zero architectural sense but looks threatening in a screenshot?” Well… the bosses look like ♥♥♥♥ though. I mean unrefined and bland,especially disappointing considering this game comes from the Castlevania bloodline of spurned developers. A series whose absolute high points were its bosses and their designs, even the PS2 entries, which were roasted in mixed flames of criticism, had that going for them.  The Gameplay Have you ever interacted with the Metroidvania product? Imagine (i cut this passage because my review is too long, well not sure someone will read it anyway and it was just an explanation of what is general considered a metroidvania SO... )  The core engagement loop of Bloodstained revolves around the Shard system,a direct evolution of the "Tactical Soul" mechanic that prioritizes the dopamine hit of random item drops over the precision of traditional action-game design. Here, the developers have introduced a dual-layer progression dichotomy: "Grade" and "Rank." By collecting duplicate Shards, the consumer increases the "Grade" (raw potency), while using the complex crafting system to upgrade the "Rank" (functionality, such as range or projectile count).  This structural depth is far more intricate than its handheld predecessors. The sheer volume of Shards ensures that approximately 80% of the available arsenal is mechanically redundant or strictly inferior to a few "meta" choices, transforming the act of experimentation into a chore of menu management. However, this dosent have to be a flaw, it could be the point of enjoyment the j-point. The "engineered experience" here is one of total dominance through preparation, allowing the consumer to essentially "out-accountant" the boss encounters through superior build optimization. Following through, the variety of weapons is astonishing. There are several types not a huge number, for sure but within each weapon type, there are many different options with special effects, like a rapier with poison or a chainsaw-like greatsword that delivers multi-hit “grinding into enemy meat” effects. Complementing this, there’s also a lot of other equipment with specific stats and unique effects, which ties into the next gameplay system: crafting.  People seem overall mixed on this, with a tendency toward negative reception of the mechanic, but I really enjoyed it having multiple ways to supply my run with different gear options felt great. This approach might be the most defining design choice, shaping the castle less like a haunted castle which is also a kind of progress bar for exploration,  and more like a crazy horror warehouse, or a horror mall, if you consider the multiple themed areas. Lol, I love that. Why am I farming flying pigs for a legendary ordinary pizza recipe? Cause Susie is Hungry and will give me a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ ring with +10 Luck for ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ fresh pizza, thats why  the soundtrack can bolster contributions by that michiru yamane, the tracks overall are not that outstanding there are some nice tracks but not that memorial, maybe one of the tracks i remember best was the one from the intro stage the „last“ area of the castle und the boss theme... on the other hand as part of the bonus modes the game brings along, there is an 8 bit redinition of several tracks which is really NEStacular!  Oh, and then there’s the story, which is (we can’t say this enough, can we) serviceable… but also pretty much typical, undercooked, and unevenly developed, with a few small moments of interesting ideas and themes. Like Miriam’s origin, the concept of the Shardbinders, or certain moments in the game where it really pulls off a hard-to-put-into-words eerie horror-survival-thriller atmosphere (which is totally intriguing in my opinion). But these moments evaporate faster than Miriam can stick crystallized monster essence into herself…  Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night, compared to contemporary genre sensations like Hollow Knight, offers a lot of gameplay variety and experimentation options, but it falls short in cohesion, atmosphere, environmental storytelling, and the elegant narrative subtlety combined with the hand-crafted charm of tight progression and level design, that makes Hollow Knight’s Hallownest such a memorable experience. Igarashi’s ritual offering of reconciliation to a fanbase that has been stuck in a time loop since 1997 is a high-quality action-adventure that feels like a labor of love from a creator or better an artisan who was finally allowed to  with all his tools at once. It’s "unsettlingly" dense and occasionally cheap-looking, but its heart is in the right place, well if that dosents sound cute i dont know either Why am I farming flying pigs for a legendary pizza recipe?

Each NPC has fun dialogue and unique side quests, there is a large amount of weapons and equipment with different effects for a large variety of builds, the upgrade system is engaging, the map/level design is interesting and well thought out with a lot of hidden nooks and crannies to explore, a mix of recurring enemies from castlevania and new enemies with unique attack patterns, boss fights feel tough but fair and are enjoyable to fight even if you're losing, the aesthetics of each area are immersive and maintain a gothic theme while still being unique from one another, and there's also a bunch of extra modes and playable characters like any castlevania game.  Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night is like Koji Igarashi took all the lessons he'd learned by working on castlevania and put it all into this one game. If you enjoyed the Iga-era Castlevania games then you'll definitely enjoy this, and even if you're not a Castlevania fan or prefer the classic era I'd still say to try it out.

Bloodstained is a visually stunning experience with a vibrant art style and impressive graphics that truly bring its gothic atmosphere to life. The gameplay is exceptionally fluid, offering smooth controls and an incredible variety of weapons and abilities that allow for a lot of creativity in how you play. However, the game does have its shortcomings, particularly with its somewhat limited cast of characters and a fairly predictable, average story. While the world feels a bit smaller than I expected for a Metroidvania, the sheer fun of the combat and the mechanical depth make it a journey well worth taking. 7.8/10

It’s incredibly fun and very engaging. The premise is also fairly interesting and well-executed. I would’ve given it a solid 7 or 8 out of 10 if it hadn’t crashed three times in a single day costing me several hours of progress.  After the first crash, I assumed it was a one-off glitch. The second was significantly worse: I had just unlocked the teleportation ray and spent about two hours revisiting previously inaccessible areas, only for the game to crash right as I was about to save. All of that progress was lost.  Even then, I kept playing because the gameplay is genuinely enjoyable.  The third crash ended that. It happened right after I cleared a difficult area and used a waystone to return to the safe house while low on resources. At that point, continuing didn’t feel worth the risk. Frequent crashes this early raise serious concerns about long-term stability, including the possibility of losing even more progress or corrupting a save file.

[h2]OBRIGATORIO[/h2]  Este é o verdadeiro sucessor espiritual de [b]Castlevania: SotN[/b]. Metroidvania da melhor qualidade, gráfico, jogabilidade, habilidades, trilha, bom demais. Game obrigatório para os fãs do gênero.

Metroidvania 10/10 eu adorei o jogo, ele é bem divertido e feito com bastante carinho pelo Koji e sua equipe, de fato ele poderia ser melhor mas eu estou muito satisfeito com o jogo, como fã de Castle eu adorei esse game pois há muitas referências.

Totalmente excelente. Divertidissimo metroidvania com bastante inspiração no Simphony of the Night, desde a mecânica de usar habilidades absorvidas de inimigos até o level design e direcão de arte puxada pro antigo gótico europeu. Bastante diversidade de gameplay com a mecânica mencionada junto com itemizacão bem flexivel e outros sistemas de crafting/receitas e melhorias de fragmentos, são realmente sistemas bem pensados, customização super divertida também. Com cenários e modelos 3D muito bonitos junto com a gameplay travada no 2D se chega num resultado bem satisfatório de olhar por várias horas, Ótimos gráficos. A trilha sonora também é bem gostosinha e memorável, excelentes composições. Fator exploração bem satisfatório via salas secretas e interligações entre o mapa mas acho que já vi mapas mais "amarrados", não é necessariamente um problema. Os bosses são todos decentes, nada incrivel ou dificil mas também não decepcionam, complementam bem o resto das fases já que geralmente são o desafio "final" da área, se tratando de inimigos esse jogo brilha bem mais nos inimigos comuns e na diversidade deles, são todos designs únicos com suas próprias particularidades e loots, bastante criatividade nesse setor (até o final que eles começam a se repetir). Platina foi meio desgraçadinha de fazer pro jogo base pq tinha uma quantidade boa de grind envolvido. pras conquistas exclusivas da DLC (Dominique's Curse) acabei por seguir um guia otimizado pra pegar tudo sem perder muito tempo, A DLC é uma continuacão da história principal com outro personagem baseada no Castlevania 2 de NES e portanto intencionalmente adota algumas "limitações" da época como a movimentação mais dura, inimigos com posições inesperadas, sistema de inventário rudimentar e no geral uma dificuldade mais difícil que definitivamente não são minha praia, mas consigo entender se o pessoal mais afeiçoado nos castlevanias clássicos se divertissem com esse conteúdo adicional, é bem feitinho.

Literally the best metroidvania I've ever played in my life. I honestly didn't think it'd get better than Metroid Dread. This game is officially in my top 3 favorites  Bloodstained is literally Castlevania in every possible way but name. Every little detail, every enemy, physics, gameplay, function, ETC. All the exact same. Reskinned and renamed  This is technically my first ever "Castlevania". If this is what they're like, I think I might like it more than Metroid. I RLY got into CV lore and binged the anime. It was so good  It genuinely breaks my heart that this game couldn't be an official Castelvania game.. This is exactly what Castlevania would've evolved into if they didn't get rid of Igarashi smh  (BTW, this is NOT a 2.5D game. 2.5D would look more like Octopath or Warhammer's Boltgun. 2D entities and characters and items in a 3D world. Bloodstained is a 3D sidescroller. There's nothing 2D about it, other than the chests and small rock models)   -----   [All Achievements]  -Lv99 -Quests: 100% -Items: 100% -Weapons: 100% -Armor: 100% -Techniques: 100% + Mastered -Demons: 100% -Shards: 100% + a lot at Rank 9 + Grade 9 -Map Completion: 100% -Food: 100% -Stats: 100% -Dominique's Curse DLC: 100%  I haven't had this much fun completing a game a while. Not a moment of it felt like a slog or a chore. I enjoyed erry second of it (Except Dominique's Curse!)  I never thought I'd ever grind in a Metroidvania as if it were another RPG, but here I am. The grind wasn't tedious at all. It was quick and easy and enjoyable   -----   [OPTIMIZATION/PERFORMANCE]  Bloodstained: RotN is EASILY the best, most optimized Unreal Engine game I've ever seen or played. I didn't know UE games could even run this well. 1080p90 at maximum possible settings on the STEAM DECK OLED, or holding WAY above 60fps  it looked like it was actually utilizing my hardware appropriately. I didn't need to manually intervene for once! [b][i]This is the first game where I can confidently say that my hardware is truly the only bottleneck here![/i][/b] And it's on Unreal Engine of all things! The optimization is SO good in this game  The optimization was NOT perfect!  Casino boss lagged. I could not reach 60fps. Once I walked in, it immediately dropped to 40fps. I had to lower my settings to minimum and play at 720p just to reach 60fps. Visually, there was nothing I could see that could cause my undervolted GPU to max out at 99% 11W. Maybe it was the coins? It only drops to 40fps when I activate the fight, so it's clearly got something to do with the boss itself  And the ending cutscene lagged hard down to 40fps, but that didn't rly matter as much  Otherwise, solid 1080p60 at max settings in every area and under any circumstance. I did crash twice   -----   [VISUALS]  The visuals were rough and flat. At max settings, at minimum. At 1080p and 720p on my OLED screen. It was rough. I thought maybe it was my 1080p display stretched out across 24 inches, but it was just as rough in 720p on my SD OLED 7" display. LIkely a bad AA method  Some objects were 2D, like some rocks and the chests. It looked weird. Kinda lazy to not make them 3D. It conflicts with the camera angle logic, being a 3D sidescroller and all. We should be seeing more defined angles the closer to the edge of the screen  Character designs were great, but they looked like clay in game  I think lighting was a very weak point of this game's visuals. It was often flat   -----   [GAMEPLAY]  VERY STIFF  When I first started playing, I was NOT impressed. The movement, the combat, the attack animations.. It felt so damb stiff. I got used to it tho. It doesn't improve, but getting new abilities like Double Jump, Accelerator, High Jump, and better weapons makes the movement significantly less insufferable. I was maneuvering through the game like a TAS (Tool Assisted Speedrun)  The backstep is dumb. I never used it  Techniques were inconvenient. Directional input combo attacks in a sidescroller is awkward. I never used them. It takes too long to perform. I'd rather bind their abilities to a single button  The quests were lame and grindy. Only 4 sets; Food for a glutton, a hit list, memorial service, and 3 quests for some bloke in a hole. They felt excessively large in quantity and excessively low in quality. The rewards sucked  The final boss sucked. It was stupid. Not fun. It sucked in both base game and Dominique's curse. Underwhelmingly lame and gay  Most of the mini bosses can easily be cheesed by being upside down. Which I don't mind, bcuz fighting them sucks in a game like this. In the speed race for the accelerator shard, you can jump over the starting point and not trigger the race. So when you run to the end and trigger that end post, you get a free shard. So I didn't actually have to do that challenge  This game was very "retro-y" with its bosses and ememies. The enemies I didn't mind; very Castlevania-esque. But the bosses, they would benefit with less basic enemy-style mechanics. The final boss, first boss, and the twin dragons were the only truly unique bosses/enemies that differed greatly from regular enemies. That's how bosses should be in any game, IMO. I'd rather have bosses that have their own custom rooms tailored to their own custom playstyle that differs greatly from each other and regular enemies   -----   [OST & STORY]  OST was amazing! Very catchy melodies  The story was mid at best. I barely paid attention. It was nothing memorable   -----   [BORKS]  In the opening cutscene, the voice was very low and was off centered towards the right side a bit. It was annoying  Epic Games kept popping up. Errytime I defeated the final boss, it'd IMMEDIATELY open the Steam browser on my Deck and go to Epic Games store. Same with finishing the boss rush  [b]You added 12 new achievements 5 years after the game released[/b]. I'm lucky I didn't play the game until now, bcuz that would've pissed me off. There's nothing worse than seeing a game disappear from your completionist list bcuz they decided to randomly add new achievements many years later. Now I gotta reinstall it. Now I gotta buy new DLC. Now I gotta play a game I rly wanted to be done with forever. ETC..  I hope, if you guys ever do this in The Scarlet Engagement, that it'll be for proper DLC. I want full expansion packs, not retro game spinoffs. I want more of the base game, but with new areas, new enemies, new story, new errything   -----   [DLC; DOMINIQUE'S CURSE]  I hated erry moment of Dominque's Curse. I've never hated playing a game so much before.. It's rage inducing. It's such a chore to play. A slog fest  Terrible physics, terrible hitboxes, often unresponsive inputs. The stairs are so stupid! Borderline unplayable. The save rooms are so far and few between, making you start ALL THE WAY from the beginning of the dungeon/castle to the boss. Oh, how many times I got fct over on my way out of the castle/dungeon, just to get sent back to the very beginning and slog thru the castle and duke AGAIN. Platforming was a nightmare. If you thought the movement was stiff AF in the base game, it's 20x worse in this one  99% of my deaths were from horrible hit boxes, it won't let me hit anything, 0 control in platforming, or inconvenient animation lock. Trying to keep up with most enemies when the mechanics are too slow, when the animation lock is stupid long. Otherwise, a very basic game. 100% of the difficulty comes from the sloppy controls and bad physics  And I get it, it's supposed to be like the OG NES games, but some things are better left in the past. I rly wish this DLC had no achievements. I hated that I even needed to buy it to complete this game  I ended up completing it in 13h+   -----   Overall, best sidescroller I ever played. Best metroidvania I ever played. I love almost errything about this game. I can't get over how fun this (the base game only) was. I am beyond hyped for The Scarlet Engagement. It genuinely looks better in erry possible way!
