A lot of water has flowed through the River Styx since one video game tore me apart like that. I want to like Bloodstained, hell, I like it, but I cannot turn blind eye from all those flaws and design mistakes that were simply pissing me off during my playthrough.   Created by Koji Igarashi, commonly known as IGA, a veteran of the video game industry, a man who invented the metroidvania genre, Bloodstained is a love letter to all those early Castlevania games from the first Playstation era. Well, what should you expect from the father of the series? And it ticks all of the checkboxes. We have a big, interconnected map, that access to some parts you gain only after obtaining certain movement abilities, a mix of platforming, combat and boss fights, skills, weapons, even very RPG-lite mechanics… It works almost flawlessly and there is little to none room for complaining about core gameplay mechanics. But on the other hand, unfortunately, nothing stands out creatively from the rest of the metroidvania games, there is nothing unique and groundbreaking. Once again I can say what I already said in the past — the genre is very formulaic, very strict with gameplay, to the point where once you played one metroidvania, it is like playing them all. If you love those games fanatically, and yearn for the same gameplay and design bits over and over again, you will be happy. If you expect something extraordinary, you will be disappointed. Because this game is generic to the core.  Story… it is serviceable. But overall it doesn't matter. You have several characters and they have motivations, agenda and needs, there is some resemble of the plot pushing everything forward, all of this presented in more traditional narration — like, dialogues or cutscenes — rather environmental storytelling, but at the end of the day, you can cut this and throw away and the game would not lose anything. The main heroine, Miriam, is bland, her motivations are generic, plot twists are predictable, and most memorable side character is Zangetsu, but only because he is voiced by David Heyter, the original voice of Solid Snake.   Okay, let’s delve a little deeper into mechanics. The core experience, aside from more traditional metroidvania mechanics, is built around items called shards. You collect them from defeated enemies and they function basically like skills and abilities. Some are just like more traditional unlockables from different games — like double jump, phasing through walls, inverting gravity — usually locked behind a boss fight. Others are more like spells, you can cast them using your mana. On the top of that we have summons, passive skills, familiars. There are more than 100 shards to collect. Or craft, because yes, this game has crafting mechanics. You can create both shards and equipment — this game also has different items you can wear, different weapon categories, armor, accessories, rings, hats… The majority of them you can craft, few basic ones can be bought in the shop. On top of all of that we have very limited, very light RPG mechanics: you kill enemies to gain experience to level up, which grants you more HP, MP and attributes boost. But forget about a more nuanced approach to building your statistics. All points are distributed automatically, so forget about different builds. You can customize your playstyle only through equipment and shards you use.  So, what problems do I have with this title? Well, there are three, all of them are connected together.   First — the game is too easy. You are locked out from higher difficulties until you beat the game first time, and the overall experience on normal is definitely unbalanced. There are certain shards and items that are definitely better then others, and due to how your stats progress, in the late game you transform into a literal Hulk-like monster, one shooting every enemy that was unlucky to cross your path. Why? Because when your attributes reach a certain threshold, your attack power and defense become so high that regular enemies cannot hurt you. I literally brute forced the entire game — pick up two handed sword, greatsword specialization passive shard, faster attack passive shard, some armor with big defense and was able to one-shot 75% enemies, while they dealt me ONE point of damage (when I have almost 2k of HP, passive regeneration shard and healing spell equipped). And the best parts — all of this stack. You can eat some food that give you a passive bonus to your strength, put some accessories, pick a book of strength, level up your passive strength shard (and when you pump them to level 9 you can use them WITHOUT equipping) and literally kill end game enemies with 3 or 4 swings. There is no main boss in this game that took me more than 5 times to defeat — some of them I destroyed on the first try. You can absolutely demolish this game if you pick up the right gear and skills.   Which leads me to the second problem — 90% of all skills and items are useless. There are more than 100 shards (like, I never used summoning shards and spell-like damaging shards. NEVER!), almost 130 different weapons, 80 accessories, 52 armor pieces, but you can ignore the majority of them and still win the game. I literally checked only if the item gave a bonus to my stats, equipped it and totally forgot about rest. If you want to motivate me to use different items, reward me in a different way than only bumping up my attributes. Why do I need to use some fancy katana with different moveset that I need to swing 5 times to kill a basic enemy, when I can use big-ass swords that kill them in one shot with proper shards? And there is no challenge here, mostly because enemies are more like obstacles than real threats. There are sections in this game where enemies stand one after another in line, so you walk to the first one, swing once to kill, move two steps forward, swing again to kill another, move forward again and repeat with the last one, because they do not even bother to put up a fight. In this game common enemies have one, maybe 2 or 3 different attacks, bosses no more then 5. You have a real problem with designed difficulty when I can kill end game enemies like dragons without taking any damage.  Lastly — the game is too grindy. I reached the last boss in 18 hours, which is decent for metroidvania, I think 20 to 25 hours is a golden standard of that type of game. Then I spend additional THIRTEEN HOURS to grind all items for 100% achievement. Not only do you grind levels, money, shards (because yes, there is no guarantee that shard will drop from the enemy, only percentage chance), but also materials for items or ingredients for food. On the top of that some shards can only drop from enemies, while others can only be crafted. ON TOP OF THAT some items require to craft other items that can only be crafted. Thankfully once you craft them, it will become available in the shop, but, yes, you need to set up an entire farming build to get enough money, or spend EVEN MORE hours grinding only pennies. I don’t understand why Japanese game developers love putting grinding into single player games. Like, you made this neat, for example, j-rpg, with a very tight main campaign clocking around 20 hours mark, and then you get a brain aneurysm and slap additional 20 hours of grinding to get the best items. Why, why, WHY do you never respect players' time? Repeating something over and over again is not gameplay, it's a chore. And if I want to do chores, I go wash some dishes, not play video games.  I will repeat myself — I like this game. But I will cut unnecessary bloat from it without blinking an eye. I will also work on difficulty to be more challenging, and lastly, on balancing. And I know, some people treating this game like second coming of Christ for the genre, because it was made by grandfather of metroidvania, but there is this one sentence that can sum all of it up perfectly:  <Look in the comment section for the rest>

年轻人的第一部恶魔城，体验有点遭。操作手感方面就不说了，只说引导和一些体验。 这游戏的引导很烂，经常是探图探完一片区域打完boss然后就不知道下一个要去哪里了，npc的对话也是跟没有一样，只有一个地图名字和怪的特征，怎么走完全不知道。比如说火车站这一段，摸完双龙塔拿到溴化银了，但是根本不知道要干嘛，交了任务拿了照片就开始迷茫，看了攻略才知道要去找吸血鬼。还有真结局拿到斩月之后，听npc描述就只知道另一个恶魔会在旁边，于是我就开始和吉贝尔转了十分钟一点反应都没有，看了攻略才知道要把boss留到残血砍月亮，真的服了。也许对于老恶魔城玩家很好理解，但对于新人就是完全的失败。 然后是跑图体验，部分场景又空又大，但横向机动只有走路、滑铲和斜下冲，经常是路上小怪一个法术就秒了但是走路走半天，点名巨兽巢穴这个鬼地方。部分场景的引导也很神秘，知道手能拿巨石和书架是因为它们边缘有高亮，但铁处女没有高亮却也能拿，导致我基本上除了最后三个地图其他都摸完了还是在瞎逛，看了攻略才知道铁处女后面原来早就能走了。 接下来是物品获取，这游戏有一个怪物图鉴，但是只有名称和碎片是否获取的，至于它会掉什么则完全没有记录，想刷个之前见过的材料都无从下手。 之后就是演出。这游戏的演出只能用难绷来形容，所有过场动画就是一个人摆pose然后放两句垃圾话就结束了，唯一有点内容的是真结局打完修女，但偏偏这个时候弹出一个关联epic账号的弹窗把内容挡住了，真不知道该怎么说。 当然也不是没有优点，地图设计还是非常不错的，双龙之塔的boss战也很惊艳，但这些依旧无法盖过其他部分给我带来的反感。

本質上是惡魔城月下夜想曲＋曉月圓舞曲的合體之作，只是換皮重現。對於想重溫惡魔城的老玩家可能很感動，但若用現代標準檢視，缺點倒相當明顯。  1. 武器與裝備太多，但絕大多數都用不到，基本上好用的就那幾個。法術亦然。 2. 雞肋的工藝與料理系統＋選單地獄。料理系統又和角色數值掛勾，基本上不能不碰，但缺乏現代的 QOL 機制（比如還缺哪些材料、缺多少、哪裡打），導致需要花很多時間查資料、記資料，反覆 backtracking 與刷怪只為了收集某些稀有素材。這類遊戲的樂趣應該在探索，而不是農怪收集材料。 3. 以 2019 年的遊戲而言，美術與畫面表現並不好。醜 3D 倒不如用月下的 2D 華麗哥德風格呈現會更精緻。 4. 遊戲中有許多美術場景物件、敵人是群眾募資 Kickstarter 的「酬庸」（？），造成風格上嚴重斷裂，能理解作為群眾募資 Kickstarter 遊戲的難處，第一次或偶爾看到會覺得有趣，但實在太多，我認為不是理想做法。  只能說，曉月圓舞曲比較好玩。雖然它也有第一點的問題，但至少節奏緊湊、簡單好玩。 這就是一款情懷之作。如果少了情懷與 IGA 名製作人的光環，作為一款 2019 的現代遊戲，我想評價會毀譽參半。
80%以上的时间全都是垃圾时间，本质刷刷刷的傻逼游戏刷到最后成长的也只有数值跑图又臭又长篝火不能传送，传送门少大地图没有辨识度别的不说我打开地图你至少把地名给我标出来吧我操机动性跟残疾人一样跑那个傻逼地图，指引也跟狗屎一样真是臭不可闻的傻逼电子古董

Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night foi um dos melhores metroidvanias que eu já joguei.  Como fã da série Castlevania, foi muito satisfatório ver o trabalho de Koji Igarashi nesse jogo. A inspiração em Castlevania: Symphony of the Night é muito clara, e para quem gosta desse estilo de jogo isso é praticamente um presente.  Um dos pontos que eu mais gostei foi a exploração. O jogo consegue guiar o jogador muito bem sem precisar ficar segurando sua mão o tempo todo. Eu consegui jogar praticamente o jogo inteiro sem precisar procurar guias ou tutoriais na internet. Para quem já está acostumado com metroidvania, a progressão flui de forma muito natural e isso torna a experiência muito mais satisfatória.  Outro ponto forte é a grande variedade de armas e itens. A protagonista Miriam tem acesso a muitos tipos de armas diferentes: botas, chicotes, pistolas, katanas, espadas longas e espadas pesadas. Cada arma muda bastante o estilo de combate. Além disso, muitas armas possuem habilidades especiais, o que deixa a experimentação ainda mais divertida. Testar novos equipamentos e descobrir como cada arma funciona faz parte da diversão do jogo.  Claro que o jogo não é perfeito. Durante minha jogatina encontrei alguns bugs, mas nada que realmente tenha estragado minha experiência. Curiosamente, um deles até acabou me ajudando a progredir em um momento do jogo.  No geral, tive uma experiência muito positiva. Se você gosta de metroidvanias ou é fã de Castlevania, eu recomendo muito dar uma chance.Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night foi um dos melhores metroidvanias que eu já joguei.

Пройдено мной на «плохую концовку» и, если честно, к финалу игра меня просто задушила. В начале всё круто: приятная боёвка, куча шардов, можно собрать интересный билд и чувствовать себя демоническим терминатором, замок красивый, исследовать интересно. Но чем ближе к концу, тем больше начинается беготни. Чтобы добраться до нужного места - пробеги полкарты до телепорта, потом ещё кусок пешком, потом пойми, что тебе нужна другая способность, и повтори заново. Оставшиеся ~20% карты так и висят, потому что желания вычищать всё нет вообще. Для метроидвании это странно - жанр про исследование, а не про марафоны по одним и тем же коридорам. В целом игра хорошая, видно наследие классических «кастлваний», атмосфера и прокачка тащат, но под конец устаёшь. На вторую часть посмотрю, но очень хочется, чтобы разработчики сделали перемещение менее душным. Пока что - скорее рекомендую, чем нет, но с оговорками.

This is the only metroidvania game I’ve played and what a wonderful introduction to the genre. I loved this game so much I’ve played through the entire thing twice and had a blast both times.  The fact you can get powers from literally every enemy in the game that gives a unique buff or skill is amazing. There’s a ton of different weapons to find, buy and craft to experiment with and there’s so much to see and explore.  Speaking of crafting, normally I hate that in games but here’s its strangely addicting. I’ve never really had a game where I willingly go scale a cathedral to kill a bird monster that has a 20% to drop apples because I need to make some apple pie dammit!  The only negative I can possibly say is that it can be one of those “Where do I go?”games. I did had to look up where I needed to go twice and both times I needed some really obscure power or armor to get to a new area which I get this game likes it’s hidden collectibles…but to hide things for story progression? Slightly annoying.   Even if you’ve never played a metroidvania, give this a shot. It’s well worth it.

[h2]教科書級別的類銀河戰士惡魔城遊戲--《血咒之城：暗夜儀式》個人主線通關感想[/h2]  筆者是昨天晚上打通的這款《血咒之城：暗夜儀式》的主線劇情的，共花費了差不多28個小時。由於很喜歡這款的探索和戰鬥，所以就特地跑了個100%地圖和100%碎片（怪物技能）獲取。但這還遠遠不是此款遊戲的全部內容，製作組用足夠多的內容展現了他們的誠意和對“惡魔城”系列的愛。     只能說，不愧是惡魔城系列出品人--五十嵐孝司所監製的“類銀”，雖然礙於“惡魔城”系列的版權在Konami（科樂美）手上而無法直接出續作，但親上手，實機遊玩後就能輕易地感覺到那股純正的“惡魔城”味：那個滑鏟，那個後撤步，那個“真空刃”，那個“一開始展現的BOSS並不是最終的BOSS”；一切都是那麼的對味，那麼的“教科書”般的優秀。特別是對筆者這種喜歡GBA上“惡魔城：曉月圓舞曲”這作的玩家來說，《血咒之城：暗夜儀式》的“碎片/水晶”系統更是“最熟悉的陌生人”：多種多樣，絕不敷衍的怪物技能給予了玩家的極其自由和豐富的戰鬥選擇，讓每一次在新地圖遇到新怪物時都能保持驚喜，想要嘗試。  探索有趣，戰鬥爽快，怪物多樣，技能繁多，武器更是五花八門。這樣的高質量的“類銀河戰士惡魔城遊戲”真的就是這個品類的標桿。筆者強烈建議任何喜歡“類銀”，但又沒玩過本作的諸位一定要來試試這款。更不要說，這款經常於steam促銷季以2.5折的價格出售，以區區TWD 317元的價格就能玩到這麼優秀的，讓人身心愉悅，投入到純粹快樂的“類銀”遊戲，實在是太過超值了。在這樣的價格下，遊戲後期有些怪物的掉寶率低下的問題，筆者是完全可以包容的。而且，開發組在做完主線的同時，後續還有在持續更新免費的DLC：陸續增加了第二個、第三個可遊玩角色，BOSS RUSH模式，競速模式，經典模式，甚至對戰模式。可以說是，相當相當的良心了。這麼好的製作組，不支持一下嗎？   最後，筆者已經迫不及待的想要在今年玩到正統續作《血咒之城：緋紅誓約》了。期待到時候獲得更多純粹的快樂~  P.S.這款遊戲最大的不足應該就是簡中和繁中的官方翻譯太垃圾了。筆者在這邊提供一份民間大佬的用熱愛鑄就的文本優化補丁：直接Google搜索關鍵字 "keylol 官方中文優化補丁"，第一個鏈接裡就是。下載後，解壓到遊戲目錄，覆蓋替換即可。

You're stuck after getting Double Jump? Well guess what, you need to know how to Jump Kick, and if you didn't know then ♥♥♥♥ you because that's what the game's telling you.  After you Double Jump, press jump again + down to kick downards and fall quick. You can do this diagonally as well by pressing left/right. You can also jump again if you kick an enemy or a lamp/candle.  After you get Double Jump, visit those areas you couldn't reach, and if you see an area you still can't reach, see if you find an enemy or a reachable lamp, Jump Kick on it and keep going.  Also, when you get to a water section and you see an enemy attacking you like a torpedo, you have to farm their shard so you can move underwater with that torpedo because YOU WON'T GET THE METROIDVANIA UPGRADE TO WALK ON WATER UNTIL LATER.  YOU NEED TO KNOW THIS TO PROGRESS THE GAME AND THAT'S ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥.  Play the game with these tips, don't waste 6 hours of your life trying to find a way to progress like I did in all my previous metroidvanias/igavanias I played for years.

最近Bloodstained: Ritual of the Nightをクリアし、またとても楽しめました。 今年に新たな作品が出るとのことですので、楽しみにしています。  一部の投稿で、新作はPC版では出ないと受け取れる書き込みがありましたが、WEB上を検索したところ「PS5/XSX|S/PC向けに2026年発売予定」との記載がありましたので安心しました。 https://www.gamespark.jp/article/2025/06/05/153433.html  なお今更言っても間に合わないかと存じますが、もし次回作も検討なさっているのでしたら、以下の意見・要望もご一考いただけると幸いです。  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ 【意見・要望】 ・故　三浦建太郎さんのベルセルクとコラボレーションして「髑髏の騎士」を登場させ、Boss として戦えるようにすると共に、プレイアブルキャラにしてほしい。 ・装備品の性能を維持しつつ、好きな見た目に変更できる様にしてほしい。 ・頭装備の表示非表示を可能にしてほしい。 ・本編クリア後に解放される追加ダンジョンと多数のBossを追加してほしい。 　※別のコンテンツとしてではなく、本編上の寄り道要素として、高難易度ダンジョンを追加してほしい。 ・追加ダンジョンでしか獲得できない装備やアイテムを追加してほしい。 ・エンドコンテンツとして、全ての装備項目に、ユーザーが成長させることのできる武具を追加してほしい。 　※属性、耐性、各種ステータス、特殊効果などを、アイテムを食わせて追加していき、最強の武具を作成できる様にしてほしい。 　※見た目はデフォルトで備わっているもの以外に、他の武器から転写もできる様にしてほしい。 　※属性ごとに、それにあったエフェクトが発生するようにしてほしい。（火なら赤、雷なら紫、など） ・複数のプレイアブルキャラクターを追加し、戦闘中に即座に切り替えながら戦えるようにしてほしい。 　※魂をリンクさせているなどの設定にすれば、HPとMPを共有していることにできかと存じます。 　※キャラクターは性能に差を設けず、好きなキャラを好きにカスタマイズして、バトルに参加できる様にしてほしい。 　（性能が足を引っ張りバトルに出せない状況は、他のゲームでもよくある事ですが、これはとても残念な気持ちになります） ・ポリコレだけは絶対に取り入れないでほしい。 　※ゲームにお金を払う層は、あのような行き過ぎた左翼思想にウンザリしています。 ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------  五十嵐孝司さんは、コナミ退社理由に「自身が本当に作りたいゲーム、特にファンが求めている探索型2Dアクションを制作する自由を得るため」とおっしゃっていたことをWEBで確認しました。  私はそういう信念と気概のある行動を取れる人に好感を抱きます。 これからも応援していますので、頑張ってください。

Very competent metroidvania.  As its name implies, it's a spiritual successor to Castlevania: Symphony of the Night made by the same director. It shows: the game not only adheres faithfully to the formula established by its predecessor, but also closely mirrors its overall feel, art style, and musical direction.  It takes inspiration from one of the all-time greats in the genre and does so effectively. The familiar and satisfying blend of well-tuned combat, platforming, exploration, and progression is all here, supported by a consistently enjoyable soundtrack.  A particular highlight is the sheer number of gameplay tools available (equipment, spells, skills, and more) which encourage experimentation and make moment-to-moment gameplay engaging.  I enjoyed most of my time with the game, largely thanks to its gameplay, though it does have its fair share of issues.  First, the game adheres too closely to its predecessor, sometimes to its detriment:  - The map is devoid of useful information. It could have displayed far more to aid navigation (such as closed doors, narrow passages, important landmarks, or water areas). This would have spared a lot of frustrating backtracking and aimless wandering. It’s hard to see how a more informative map would have harmed the experience, making this feel like a case of blind copy-pasting from older Castlevania titles. - There are no autosaves. I frequently found myself detouring to save rooms “just to be safe” (especially since save rooms also heal you). In a game heavily focused on exploration, these constant detours disrupt the pacing. While I understand the desire to preserve challenge by avoiding frequent autosaves, a middle ground could have worked. For example, autosaving when entering new castle areas and teleport rooms (teleport rooms already allow access back to the village and its save room, so this would have preserved the intended difficulty while cutting down on wasted time).  Beyond these inherited issues, the game also has problems of its own:  - The addition of a hub village with NPC shopkeepers and quest-givers feels underdeveloped. Quests are often tedious or grind-heavy, with rewards that frequently fail to justify the effort. - There is a sharp difficulty spike near the very end. On normal difficulty, the game feels well balanced until the final two boss fights, which are disproportionately hard and forced me to stop progressing and grind for hours. It comes across as an artificial way to extend playtime and feels out of place. - The game grants its best movement ability very late. Given the sparse map information and the amount of farming required for quests, a large amount of time is spent running back and forth. Access to faster movement earlier would have greatly improved the experience. - The story is poorly written and highly predictable. While this is a genre where gameplay rightly takes priority, the game lingers a bit too long, especially early on, on dialogue and exposition in service of lore and characters that are largely uninteresting.  Fortunately, these flaws don’t undermine the core gameplay. The formula remains strong, and the overall experience is highly enjoyable. Despite its issues, the game stands as a very respectable entry in the genre, and I can confidently recommend it.

Not bad, but far from perfect. Recommended for Igavania fans.  The overall gameplay is really smooth, but there are so many systems, many of which could have had a lot of potential, that are utilized once and then forgotten. You get an ability, use it once to progress, and that's about it, you'll most likely never need (or have a reason to use) that ability ever again. That was by far my biggest letdown.  The game is also really ugly. I generally don't care about graphics that much, but the 2.5D visual style just didn't really work here. Especially cutscenes and other parts where the camera zooms in highligh the ugliness.  Then there's the complete lack of originality. I mean yeah, it's Iga's own games that are being copied, but it's a bit too much. Almost everything feels like it's recycled from Symphony of the Night and Aria of Sorrow.  I don't regret playing it through, and as said, it's a decent or even good Igavania, but I doubt I'm going to touch it again.

Fantastic metroidvania that’s a joy to explore. The environments are engaging, the enemies are creatively designed, and the boss fights are thrilling n challenging. I really enjoyed the story, and the voice acting is top-notch, with even David Hayter delivering a great performance.  Unlocking new weapons and shards adds a great sense of progression, making traversal and exploration even more satisfying. I didn’t dive too deeply into the side content, collecting shards, recipes, or filling out the encyclopaedia—as it was to much filler for me. In hindsight, that probably left me underpowered for much of the game, because the main bosses absolutely destroyed me. I had to replay several of them an absurd number of times.  And the final boss? Don’t get me started. I spent hours learning attack patterns and slowly chipping away at an enormous health bar but i was buzzing when I finally beat it.  Despite the difficulty spikes, I genuinely loved my time with it. The characters and story really stood out, and overall a difficult but rewarding experience

I am an original Kickstarter backer of this title.  I played this game to completion on my PlayStation 4 version that I received for backing the game.  I used to whole-heartedly recommend it, and it used to bring me immense joy.  I have recently learned that the December update in 2025, two days before Christmas, added Epic Online Services to this title for the purposes of crossplay with the Epic Game Store version of the game.  I do not buy games from Epic Game Store for a variety of reasons, and forcing their middleware into another store's version of the game six years after release is despicable behavior.  I am afraid that this marks the end of my support of Bloodstained and ArtPlay.   If you wish to honor Shutaro iida's memory like I do, please consider playing Castlevania: Aria of Sorrow, Dawn of Sorrow, Portrait of Ruin, or any of the other excellent titles that he worked on.   Shame on you, ArtPlay. Shame on you, 505 Games.  Do Not Recommend.

Jogo maravilhoso, puta madre. A gameplay desse jogo é lisinha e bem fluida, é realmente muito bom andar e bater com essa boneca. A exploração desse jogo é espetacular. Eu quero perguntar pra pessoa que construiu esse castelo o que ela usou pra fazer tamanha perfeição. As áreas desse jogo são muito diversificadas, brother. Temos desde áreas convencionais de um Castlevania, como jardim, entrada e caverna, até um deserto e uma parte toda oriental. Cada área desse jogo é única e linda de se ver. Gostei também de coisas mais simples da exploração, como as cadeiras jogadas pelo cenário que só servem pra apreciar a beleza da personagem, ou o piano no jardim que toca uma linda melodia. Falando em linda, vamos falar da música desse jogo. Mano… se a pessoa que fez a música desse jogo estiver solteira, eu peço ela em casamento, porque, nossa senhora, essa pessoa é perfeita. A trilha sonora é fantástica, não tem como colocar em palavras o quão bem feitas essas músicas são, é só ouvindo mesmo. A minha favorita é “Gears of Fortune”, aquele violino é do caralho. Gostei bastante das mecânicas desse jogo, seja a de fragmentos, os crafts e, principalmente, a parte de cozinhar. Eu normalmente odeio cozinhar em jogos, porque geralmente a recompensa é desanimadora: ou a comida só cura, ou dá um buff temporário que acaba em poucos minutos. Mas aqui o buff é permanente, e isso é muito bom, porque faz você querer ir atrás dos ingredientes pra ficar mais forte. Gostei bastante também da variedade de armas. Tem até como usar arma de fogo, o que foi uma novidade bem interessante. Esse jogo também tem quests, que são ok. Não são difíceis, estão ali e faz quem quer. Outra coisa que eu adorei foi a personalização da personagem principal. Tem um número legal de acessórios que mudam a aparência da boneca. Também dá pra mudar o corte de cabelo da personagem e pintá-lo, o que é outra mecânica bastante divertida. A única coisa ruim disso tudo é que não temos nenhuma roupa ou armadura diferente, é só a principal mesmo, e, porra, bem que podia. Os bosses desse jogo são divertidos pra caralho, tirando um ou outro que é mediano. Tem muito boss incrível, cada um com uma mecânica única e divertida. Seja a Bloodless, com sua mecânica de sangue e cura (que, nossa senhora, que piranha do caralho), os dragões gêmeos com uma bossfight bem interessante, a doppelganger que usa as mesmas habilidades que a gente, o bicho que parece um super sentai, a Gremory, que também é legal, e o Bael, que é maneiro pra caramba. Amei os bosses desse jogo, genuinamente incríveis. Gostei bastante também das referências a Castlevania, sendo a minha favorita a do pachinko que representa a Konami, puta merda, isso arrancou uma risada sincera minha. Também tem o vampiro O.D., que podemos fazê-lo quicar da cadeira, o que é bastante divertido. Os gráficos desse jogo foram uma ótima surpresa. Pensei que iria odiá-los por serem em 3D, mas catapimbas, eles são incrivelmente lindos. Fizeram um ótimo uso do 3D aqui. Outra coisa que vale comentar é o uso genial da perspectiva, que ficou incrível, principalmente na parte da torre dos dragões gêmeos. Mesmo que o mapa fique um pouco esquisito, ainda assim é do caralho. A história é legalzinha. Não tenho muito o que comentar sobre ela: funciona, tá ali, temos algumas interações engraçadas da Miriam e alguns momentos maneiros, e é isso. Só pra deixar claro: eu adorei a Miriam. Agora, o que eu não gostei foi pouca coisa. O jogo é fácil demais. Genuinamente, se você explorar o castelo, fazer itens e cozinhar, o jogo fica easy demais. Claro que você não pode jogar como um imbecil, mas ainda assim é fácil. O grind excessivo também me incomodou. Tudo nesse jogo tem que grindar, meu Deus. Tipo, o grind é fácil, tudo dropa com certa facilidade, mas mesmo assim… pra quê? Ia doer a mão dos caras se espalhassem alguns itens pelo mapa? A área final não é ruim, mas é sem graça pra caralho. Os monstros só ficam gigantes, a área é meio foda-se, muito sem sal. Bom, no final digo que amei o jogo. E pra quem ainda está em dúvida se compra ou não, irmão… tem um botão dedicado pra boneca fazer JoJo pose. Se isso não é perfeito, eu não sei o que é. Nota: 10/10

Tl;dr: A great Metroidvania, with some fantastic new additions to the formula, held back by too much fluff, inconsistent enemy designs and art direction, and a convoluted final act that begins to lose itself. It's still very much worth playing for any side scrolling action fan.  I wish Bloodstained could've been the game to make me say "Castlevania's back!" It's got what you love about the metroidvania entries of the series: fast-paced, dodge-while-striking action with RPG itemization and a variety of skills (shard in this game) at your disposal. However, there is too much of a good thing, as the shards and equipment pretty quickly get into the realm of silly and useless for the sake of just having more. Buzzword game mechanics like crafting are also thrown in here haphazardly. The final act is incredibly convoluted to get through and you are rewarded for your struggles with some of the worst zones in the game.  + The core gameplay is as good as its ever been in a Metroidvania + "Twin-stick" Directional Spells/shards might be the most fun combat addition to ever be added to a Metroidvania + Some truly exciting and fun non-combat abilities to unlock like Accelerator and Invert + Overall solid bosses + Some really nice uses of 2.5D + Upgrading Shards makes finding your favorites even more exciting + Most of the zones in the game are varied and fun  - There's just too much. So many shards and items that are either useless or just silly and break the atmosphere. Cooking for stat buffs is just a hat on a hat. - Speaking of breaking the atmosphere, the enemy designs are all over the place. Some referencing other video games while others are just photorealistic puppy heads.  - Enemy placement falls off in the latter half of the game. It just starts filling the room and becomes a chore to fight, instead of an excitingly fast and dangerous pace. - Metroidvanias are known for having their "fake finales". While most of these are simply "fill out the map and you'll find the items you need to equip", Bloodstained goes truly buck-wild with its convolution. You'll be required to pretty much comb over every inch of the map 1-3 times after getting a new ability to find exactly where to use it to get the strange exact thing you need to move a little further in. A guide is heavily advised and that SUCKS. - Cooking, crafting, and even gardening are shoved into this game, because those are the "in" features to have in every single game now. Opening chests to just get ingredients or components is so disheartening and it's such an unnecessary time-consumer. - Once you get the invert skill and progress to the final act's zones, it's like they forget how to design levels. So many platforms are JUST out of reach of the double jump, forcing you to cumbersomely do a double invert just get over a normal ledge.   Overall I think Bloodstained is a great Metroidvania that just went a little to wild with its Kickstarter-funded development. A lot of doing things because they could and not because they should. What could've been a tight, incredible return to form with some truly amazing additions to the formula, is instead bogged down by shoving in ill-fitting game mechanics, weird enemy designs, pointless weapons and shards, and one of the worst "hidden acts" I've experienced.  Still, it's a really freaking fun video game.   Overall Rating: Good (Metroidvania)

It's hard to talk about Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night without talking about Castlevania: Symphony of the Night.  Symphony of the Night is one of my favorite games of all time. It's addictive; it really hooked me. There's a flow state of exploration, killing enemies, finding treasure and secrets that the game is adept at keeping you in. The pixel art, gameplay, music, sound effects, even the cheesy voice acting all comes together in one glorious experience. It's the reason "vania" is in "metroidvania." The term metroidvania refers to 2D platformers that have a large explorable map which you backtrack through with new powers later on in the game. Symphony of the Night, along with Super Metroid, are the two games which inspired the entire genre. From 1997 when SotN released, until 2008 when Castlevania: Order of Ecclesia released, there were 7 Castlevania games released in this style.  However, after 2008, the Castlevania franchise no longer received any more metroidvania games. Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night is an attempt to pick the torch back up after Konami dropped it. Castlevania's former lead producer, Koji Igarashi, opened a Kickstarter campaign to fund the development of a Castlevania successor, which was well funded and led to Bloodstained.  Bloodstained is a 2D metroidvania platformer, although the assets are all 3D. I prefer the original pixel art of Symphony, but for the most part the 3D assets look decent, although some are rougher than others. The gameplay consists of standard metroidvania stuff, run around a big map (castle here, although there are some late game zones that don't make a lot of sense in terms of placement), get new powers (double jump of course), kill enemies. The metroidvania loop of explore, get stronger, find stuff is here in full force and it's as addictive as ever. There is a shard system in this game similar to Aria/Dawn of Sorrow which lets you absorb enemy abilities; it's a lot of fun to use the different powers and get completely broken. There are also quite a lot of different weapon and item types and a surprisingly huge crafting system. Special shout out to the music, lots of really catchy tracks that got stuck in my head. Although, the boss theme is usually always the same, which is a little disappointing.  This game definitely succeeds in its goal of feeling like the "metroidvania" style Castlevania games. It feels very much like a modern Symphony of the Night (although I think SotN still holds up well today). The music, art, sound effects, gameplay, all are very reminiscent of SotN. I've played a decent amount of metroidvanias, but none really come close to nailing the Castlevania feeling, except this.  I am very glad this game exists thanks to Kickstarter, and I had a great time with it, but there are two reasons that I think Bloodstained doesn't rise above its inspirations: it is too disjointed, and it is too derivative.   The crowdfunding aspect of the game is quite apparent at times, and the game is much less cohesive for it. It's great that so many people loved the old metroidvania style Castlevanias and funded the campaign, but campaign rewards that change some sort of thing in-game have to be carefully managed, lest they start getting too wacky. There are bosses, enemies, items, and rooms that just don't fit in the world, making it quite immersion-breaking, for lack of a better term. Some examples are a massive puppy head enemy that is in a train station for some reason, and a secret boss that is a slot machine. There is an undercurrent of disjointedness running through the whole game which definitely detracts from the overall package.  The similarity to Symphony of the Night (and the metroidvanias in the franchise afterwards) is Bloodstained's biggest blessing and its greatest curse. Bloodstained cannot exist without Symphony of the Night. Of course, it's a spiritual successor, but it feels almost too self-referential (as far as it can be self-referential as a different franchise). It feels like an off-brand Castlevania a lot of the time, because it is. As fun as it can be, the shadows of its ancestors are always looming large over it. Many of the powers, characters, and designs feel like they were ripped straight from Castlevania and slapped with a new coat of paint (medusa head -> dullahammer head). This does wonders for making Bloodstained feel like a successor, but it means the game never quite outgrows its roots. Everything that makes Bloodstained fun is taken from something that came before. It's hard to describe the exact feeling, but it feels like the game is calculated to hit all the right notes, tug at the nostalgia, and get you thinking "wow just like the old games." Not every game has to be innovative or completely original, but it feels a bit shameless in this case. Bloodstained doesn't feel like it can stand on its own.  Aside from my more complicated feelings, I still like Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night quite a lot. I'd recommend it to anyone who likes metroidvanias, and if you don't already, it might not be a bad starting point. However, I think the people who will appreciate this game the most are the ones who already know what they're getting into, and want to scratch that same old itch.  Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night is a decent successor, but unfortunately, if I had to pick one game, I'd still take Castlevania: Symphony of the Night over it any day.

Un vibrant hommage à Castelvania, symphony of the night de ps1, de nombreuses références et clins d'oeil à ce hit de 97.  Tout est travaillé et soigné, l'aspect graphique, la musique, les décors, la variété de loots, salles et boss secrets. On sent dans Bloodstained tout l'amour du Metroid-castelvania-like (et surtout de SOTN) de la part des développeurs.  Je vais évidemment faire le DLC Classic mode II, car je trouve l'aspect Castelvania NES très bien fait.

[h1][b]「『月下』の夜想曲は終わらない。―かつての魔王（IGA）が再び築き上げた、我らが望んだ『城』がここにある」[/b][/h1]  [h1]ストーリー[/h1]  18世紀のイギリス。錬金術師の呪いによって、身体が結晶化していく秘術をかけられた少女ミリアム。  彼女が10年の昏睡から目覚めた時、かつての仲間ジーベルは悪魔を召喚し、地獄の城を現世に出現させていた。  ミリアムは自らの体に埋め込まれた結晶（シャード）の力を駆使し、人類を、そしてかつての友を救うために城へと乗り込む。  [h1]概要[/h1]  『悪魔城ドラキュラX 月下の夜想曲』などを手掛けた五十嵐孝司（IGA）氏による、探索型2D横スクロールアクションRPG（メトロイドヴァニア）  Kickstarterで圧倒的な支援を集めて開発された本作は、広大な悪魔城の探索、敵の能力を奪う「シャード」システム、豊富な武器と装備の収集・合成（クラフト）要素を備えた、まさに「Igavania（イガヴァニア）」の集大成。 音楽には山根ミチル氏も参加しており、ゴシックホラーの世界観を完璧に彩っている。  [h1]感想[/h1]  [b]「これだよ、俺たちが待ち望んでいたのは！」と叫びたくなる、実家のような安心感。[/b]  コナミが新作を作らなくなり、飢えていたファンにとって、本作はまさに「約束された神ゲー」だった。  プレイした瞬間に感じる、あの独特の操作感。バックステップの挙動、ダメージ数値のフォント、そして破壊できる壁の中に隠された肉。すべてが『月下』や『暁月』の遺伝子を色濃く受け継いでおり、それでいてUnreal Engine 4による美しい2.5Dグラフィックで現代風にアップデートされている。  特筆すべきは「シャード（魂）」システムの楽しさだ。  倒した敵から確率で能力を入手できるため、「こいつの魂が出るまで部屋を出入りする」という、あの懐かしくも中毒性の高い作業に没頭してしまう。武器種も剣、鞭、銃、刀と豊富で、自分だけの戦闘スタイルを構築できる自由度の高さは、時間を忘れて城を彷徨わせてくれるだろう。  [h1]評価[/h1]  【GOOD】  ・「探索→能力獲得→行ける場所が増える」というメトロイドヴァニアの黄金律を完璧に踏襲しており、探索の快感が最後まで持続する。  ・敵の能力を奪うシャード収集や、素材を集めて装備を作るハクスラ要素の中毒性が高く、やり込み甲斐がある。  ・山根ミチル氏による荘厳なBGMと、ゴシックホラーの美しい背景美術が、耽美な世界観を見事に表現している。  【BAD】  ・一部のエリアやボスの難易度バランスが大味で、強力なシャードや回復アイテムを大量に持ち込めばゴリ押しできてしまう（良くも悪くもRPG的）  ・「お使いクエスト（特定の敵を倒せ、アイテムを持ってこい）」の内容が単調で、報酬のためだけに作業的にこなす必要がある。  ・Switch版ほどではないが、PC版でも特定のエリアやエフェクトが重なる場面で稀に処理落ちやクラッシュが発生することがある（現在は概ね改善されている）

4/5 Un buon metroidvania nel senso più classico del termine, essendo un chiaro omaggio a Castelvania in molti dei suoi aspetti. Il gioco ha una discreta varietà di livelli, nemici e segreti, nonché una marea di oggetti, collezionabili e oggetti di crafting per veramente tutti i gusti. Già solo le armi sono 10 tipi diverse per un totale che sarà quasi sul centinaio. Ovviamente è possibile godersi il gioco ignorando tutte le sidequest e il crafting, poiché il gioco è piuttosto facile: a livello normale si muore pochissimo e a parte uno dei primi boss gli altri li ho battuti tutti al primo tentativo. Il completismo invece è forse un po' esagerato, perché molti degli oggetti necessari sono rarissimi e ottenibili dai nemici con percentuali minime (1-4%, seppure incrementabile), portando solo un noioso grinding. Per il resto è un gioco molto curato, anche se per i miei gusti nello stile troppo "giapponese" nei suoi aspetti, storia e personaggi. Nel complesso comunque un titolo assolutamente valido, di media durata ma con tanti extra da esplorare per gli appassionati. Costa un po', ma in sconto sicuramente è da prendere.

7.0/10 [b] (Latest EOS Edition) [/b] [h3] IGA: ...and it's time for a resurrection! ; Me: Don't resurrect—just stay buried. [/h3] [b] Instead of wasting time on this game, you'd be better off revisiting Castlevania. [/b] I deliberately waited until 2026—nearly seven years after the game's initial release—to play it, mainly hoping for fixes to its numerous critical bugs and for IGA to fulfill his promise of free DLC updates. Unfortunately, even today, the game remains plagued by a significant number of critical bugs that severely impact the experience [b] (e.g., “fatal error” crashes, save file corruption, texture glitches, text errors, etc.) [/b]. Moreover, the overall quality of the free DLC updates is mediocre, with some offering downright terrible experiences. Worse still, certain achievements are tied to paid DLC, meaning you must spend more money to unlock the full set.  Starting from IGA's crowdfunding campaign in May 2015, the project ultimately raised $5.5 million, if memory serves. It's fair to say Bloodstained's eventual release was a miracle born from the collective efforts of true Castlevania fans worldwide. Yet the development journey was far from smooth, fraught with setbacks: originally promised for early 2017, the game suffered two major delays before launching in mid-2019 as an incomplete, bug-ridden half-baked product. Furthermore, Wii U, PS Vita, Linux, and Mac versions were successively canceled that year. After support for some platforms (like PS Vita, Linux, and Mac) was withdrawn, publisher 505 Games refused refunds to backers, claiming funds had been exhausted—a move that left some players disappointed even then. Even a year after its launch, the game remained plagued by numerous critical bugs, including errors in the Japanese text, while the free DLC promised by IGA remained nowhere to be found. In either 2021 or 2022, the achievement system malfunctioned for months, preventing players from unlocking any accomplishments. Despite repeated reports to the developers, no timely response was ever provided. As I write this review in 2026, the game still suffers from numerous issues, compounded by the devastating blow of [b] Epic Online Services (EOS) [/b]. Setting aside nostalgia, the entire journey has been quite disappointing.  As for the game itself, when compared to all of IGA's Castlevania works released before his departure in 2014, it can only be considered a mediocre, unremarkable effort with very average overall quality. In my view, it barely qualifies as a proper Castlevania title. [b] At best, it could be called a culmination of the series; at worst, it feels like a Frankenstein's monster, haphazardly stitching together various classic elements from the franchise, ultimately losing its own identity in the process. [/b]  The female protagonist feels like a hodgepodge: [b] Shanoa's tragic heroine journey + Charlotte's childlike innocence + Alucard's sense of duty + Soma's dominion powers and Dracula reincarnation + Jonathan's weapon skills and proficiency + a Julius-like companion who wants to kill her + a Richter-like friend controlled by demons... The game itself blends elements from every classic 2D side-scrolling Castlevania title between 1993's Rondo of Blood and 2008's Order of Ecclesia [/b]. While the overall fusion is passable, and many familiar touches brought a smile to my face, this also robs the game of its own identity. It becomes a mere echo of the Castlevania series, leaving veteran players seeking only nostalgia and familiarity within it.  Perhaps due to 3D design constraints or budget limitations, [b] the game's combat mechanics feel somewhat clunky. Level design lacks memorable landmarks, enemy designs feel monotonous with excessive monster repetition, boss battles lack impact and feature unremarkable designs, and combat overall lacks that satisfying back-and-forth feel. While the soundtrack is composed by Michiru Yamane, it's generally monotonous and lacks truly memorable tracks. What disappoints me most is its map design. Connections between areas feel disjointed, and the overall layout lacks the cohesive brilliance of classic Castlevania's integrated maps [/b]. It should have either adopted a seamless map structure like Symphony of the Night, Circle of the Moon, Aria of Sorrow, or Dawn of Sorrow; or it could have adopted a level-based or zoned map structure, like Rondo of Blood, Portrait of Ruin, or Order of Ecclesia. This game's map appears unified but is actually composed of isolated, scattered zones with poor connectivity, resulting in a linear progression. Combined with monotonous environmental art, the overall experience feels forgettable and highly repetitive.  Moreover, [b] the aspect I dislike most about this game is the overwhelming amount of repetitive grinding mechanics: grinding souls, grinding materials, grinding gear, grinding quests, grinding recipes, grinding this and grinding that... [/b] This easily induces fatigue and boredom. While grinding is a hallmark of the Castlevania series, it has never been the primary focus. In past Castlevania titles, grinding elements complemented other gameplay mechanics, retained a degree of fun, and remained relatively light-hearted, making them easier for players to accept. In this game, however, the sheer volume of repetitive grinding is staggering. It no longer feels like a Castlevania signature but has instead become a grinding-focused game in its own right.  In summary, even as someone who's played Castlevania all my life, I find Bloodstained falls short of passing muster as the so-called “spiritual successor.” That said, when evaluated on its own merits, the game still possesses several commendable qualities. But precisely because I've played Castlevania my entire life, I've set aside sentimental nostalgia and approached this game with a serious, critical eye. Before playing Bloodstained, I spent over a hundred hours revisiting several classic 2D side-scrolling Castlevania titles. Transitioning directly into Bloodstained afterward made the contrast in quality starkly apparent.  [b] If you're a new player unfamiliar with this genre, I personally recommend using the 2-hour refund window to try it out. If it suits your taste, I genuinely hope you find enjoyment in it. Otherwise, I wouldn't recommend this game—instead, as mentioned at the start, I'd suggest diving straight into the original Castlevania series. [/b]

[b]Gameplay :[/b] I haven't played a lot, just enough to get through the first few zones and beat the samurai guy. I personnally didn't like how the character controls. [b]Story :[/b] Haven't gotten far enough to make an opinion about it. It seemed average, not enough to hook me.  [b]Audio :[/b] The music was alright. Here in 2026 I can't recommend this game.  If you're a fan of SOTN or the other IGA games, you'll be guilt tripping yourself the whole time for thinking this game is bland/mid. There are lesser known, flawed indie metroidvanias that run circles around this crowd-funded game. I had a lot to say while playing this game but after reading a bunch of the negative reviews, I'd essentially be repeating what they all said. I got sucked into the grinding aspect of the game until I stopped and thought "Why am I doing this? I'm sure I could make it far with the set I have now?" After thinking this and buying a bunch of healing items, I made it to the final bosses that day. So the game is EASY and can only be difficult by ANNOYING you with deliberate, trolly enemy placement and head scratching level design. You'll never play this game and think "OOOH THAT WAS CLEVER! GOOD ONE IGA!"   Speaking of IGA, I really think he was headed in the right direction with the DS games, that series of game ended with Order of Ecceslia which is arguably the best (and most challenging) game. Makes me wish he made a spiritual sequel to that game and not this nostalgia bait ass game. OoE felt like he was building on his previous knowledge of creating these games, Bloodstained feels like a huge step back. You won't see areas, enemies or bosses that'll motivate you to keep going. After dedicating several goon sessions to Dominique, I will be moving on. Farewell.

Dollar store symphony of the night, una decepción total de juego, despues de anunciarlo como que el creador y  bastante del equipo de los castlevanias lo hacia, esperaba algo muchisimo mejor, mas teniendo en cuenta que juegos como hollow knight y blasphemous son muchisimo mejores y salieron antes que este juego. El diseño de niveles es bastante simple la mayor parte del tiempo otras veces te quedas encallado por cosas que no tienen correlacion alguna, por ejemplo lo del pasaporte con el vampiro, nadie te decia absolutamente nada que el vampiro opcional que encuentras en una esquina remota del mapa que no tiene nada que ver con pasaportes, te hace uno, y el juego esta lleno de momentos como estos, el combate es super basico. Hay un monton de opciones de los poderes de los cristales pero ninguno sirve de absolutamente nada porque los mejores los obtienes casi al principio del juego y para que usar opciones peores, todas las armas de la misma clase tienen el mismo moveset por lo que alternar entre armas del mismo tipo es inutil, lo que hacia funcionar esta mecanica en dark souls por ejemplo, es que cada arma del juego tiene un moveset distinto, por lo que cada arma tenia su valor porque se adequaba a tu situacion. Y en cuanto la musica no entiendo que pasó porque Curse of The Moon tenia un OST excelente y despues del primer nivel esperaba oir las canciones de este mas desarrolladas o canciones nuevas buenas pero apenas hay buenas canciones, esperaba mucho mas de la compositora del increible OST del Symphony of the NIght. En resumen, no pierdas tu tiempo con este juego, juega hollow knight o blasphemous o cualquier otro metroidvania que no sea este.

Minha Análise de Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night  ----- Gráficos -----  🔲 Free Fire 🔲 Aceitável ✅ Bom 🔲 Ótimo 🔲 Gráfico realista 🔲 Cada pixel é uma obra-prima  ----- Requisitos -----  🔲 Qualquer coisa que conduza energia 🔲 Placas GT ✅ Roda em PC humilde 🔲 Placas GTX 🔲 Precisa de uma RTX 🔲 Uma RTX 4090 para usar tudo no ULTRA  ----- História -----  🔲 Péssima 🔲 Ruim 🔲 Dá para ignorar ✅ Boa 🔲 Fantástica 🔲 Obra-prima  ----- Jogabilidade -----  🔲 Porca! 🔲 Ruim ✅ Mediana 🔲 Bacana 🔲 Ótima 🔲 Excelente  ----- Dificuldade -----  🔲 Você escolhe a dificuldade 🔲 Fácil ✅ Médio (Não dá para escolher a dificuldade antes de zerar pela primeira vez) 🔲 Difícil 🔲 Muito difícil 🔲 Dificuldade Soulslike  ----- Tempo de Jogo -----  🔲 Da para zerar e pedir reembolso 🔲 3h ~ 12h ✅ 12h ~ 24h (História principal) ✅ 24h ~ 50h (DLC + Platina) 🔲 50h ~ 100h 🔲 100h ~ 200h+  ----- Trilha Sonora -----  🔲 Ruim 🔲 Inexistente 🔲 Mediana ✅ Boa (Algumas músicas, principalmente a de exploração, fica na cabeça por algum tempo) 🔲 Muito boa 🔲 Uma obra-prima que eleva a experiência  ----- Bugs -----  🔲 Nunca vi nenhum ✅ Pequenos bugs 🔲 Pode ficar irritante 🔲 Muitos bugs 🔲 Vai estragar o jogo 🔲 Mineirinho (Nem abre mais)  ----- Público -----  🔲 Tudo e todos ✅ Crianças ✅ Adolescentes ✅ Adultos 🔲 Sem vida social 🔲 Idosos  ----- Compensa Comprar? -----  🔲 É de graça 🔲 Melhor rasgar seu dinheiro 🔲 Não ✅ Sim (Mas espere uma promoção) -> (Melhor desconto da Steam: R$ 18,87 / -75%) 🔲 Sim! 🔲 COMPRE IMEDIATAMENTE SEM ARREPENDIMENTO!!!  [b]Review de Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night (36h – Platinado – Nota pessoal: 7/10)[/b]  Bloodstained foi um jogo que entregou aquilo que prometeu — e, ao mesmo tempo, falhou em decisões que impedem que ele seja algo realmente memorável. Após 36 horas, ficou claro que estamos diante de um metroidvania competente, carismático e claramente apaixonado pelo legado que carrega (principalmente o diretor dele, que foi um dos criadores dos jogos clássicos de Castlevania), mas também de um jogo que sofre com excesso de sistemas, escolhas de design questionáveis e um balanceamento que nem sempre conversa bem com a própria proposta. O resultado é uma experiência boa, divertida e cheia de identidade, mas que raramente atinge o nível de excelência que poderia alcançar.  A jogabilidade é sólida desde o início. O controle da Miriam é responsivo, o movimento é agradável e o jogo passa rapidamente a sensação de estar em um RPG misturado com metroidvania. Os gráficos não tentam ser realistas e nem precisam: o visual tem charme próprio, mistura bem o 2D e, às vezes, entra o 2.5D em alguns cenários, entregando áreas bonitas. Mesmo assim, em certos momentos o jogo parece tecnicamente datado (Só dá para entender quando se joga e olha alguns cenários). Ainda assim, funciona, pois é divertido.  A ambientação é um dos pontos mais fortes. O castelo é variado, com áreas que vão do gótico clássico a locais etéreos, e a exploração é constantemente recompensada com novos caminhos, segredos e passagens ocultas (o padrão dos metroidvanias, mas, ainda é algo bem legal de se ver quando bem feito). Existem muitas referências a outros jogos indies e ao próprio legado do gênero, o que torna a jornada ainda mais divertida para quem gosta de caçar esses detalhes. A trilha sonora acompanha tudo isso com competência: não é nível masterpiece, mas é marcante. As músicas de exploração e o tema do menu ficam facilmente na cabeça e ajudam a criar aquela sensação confortável de “quero explorar mais”, típica de um bom metroidvania de exploração.  O combate oferece uma variedade enorme de armas, equipamentos, fragmentos e habilidades. Espadas, lanças, chicotes, armas de fogo, livros mágicos… opções não faltam. Existem combos específicos para cada tipo de arma, embora o jogo praticamente não incentive o jogador a usá-los de verdade (são combos que exigem uma sequência de 3 a 6 botões e nem dão tanto dano assim). O sistema de fragmentos adiciona ainda mais possibilidades, permitindo builds bem diferentes, e os pets, apesar de simples, ajudam tanto em combate quanto em utilidade.  Os chefes são numerosos e visualmente interessantes, mas mecanicamente deixam a desejar. A maioria das lutas se resume a pular, desviar, bater quando dá e abrir o menu para comer (sim, tem que abrir o menu para se curar, isso tira a imersão em diversas batalhas; falarei sobre isso no próximo parágrafo); não existem grandes mecânicas, fases marcantes ou coreografias memoráveis. O início do jogo é consideravelmente mais difícil, muito por conta da escassez de recursos, equipamentos e opções de cura. Depois disso, a curva se inverte: o jogo fica fácil demais, especialmente quando o jogador descobre como farmar comida e trivializar o sistema de cura com os buffs e cristais.  E aqui entram alguns dos problemas mais irritantes. Não existe um botão de cura rápida. Toda vez que você precisa se curar, é necessário abrir o menu, navegar por uma lista enorme de itens e escolher o que usar. Isso seria menos problemático se o menu fosse mais simples, mas ele é carregado de abas, informações e submenus que você precisa acessar o tempo todo: equipamentos, fragmentos, bestiário, quests, crafting, inventário. A interface funciona, mas é pouco prática para algo que deveria ser rápido e fluido.  O mapa também deixa a desejar. Ele é funcional, mostra NPCs, salas importantes, fast travel e save points, mas visualmente é básico demais. Tudo é azul, não há nomes de áreas ao navegar e existe apenas um tipo de pino para marcação (e ele é feio), ainda por cima limitado em quantidade. Em um jogo que incentiva tanto a exploração, backtracking e habilidades bloqueadas, isso soa como uma oportunidade desperdiçada. Ter diferentes tipos de marcadores, talvez até compráveis nas lojas, faria muita diferença.  A progressão sofre bastante com o sistema de morte. Se você morrer sem salvar, perde não apenas itens e fragmentos, mas também o progresso do mapa daquela área. Isso obriga o jogador a salvar o tempo todo e gera situações absurdas, como derrotar um chefe importante, avançar pelos inimigos normais na exploração e, se morrer depois, ter que enfrentá-lo novamente como se nada tivesse acontecido. Não há justificativa narrativa ou de design que torne isso aceitável, especialmente em um jogo focado em exploração.  O grind é outro ponto problemático. No mid e late game, o jogo começa a exigir quantidades exageradas de dinheiro e itens com pouca probabilidade de drop para o crafting, tornando o processo repetitivo e cansativo. O sistema de craft, que no início é interessante, vira algo mecânico e pouco recompensador depois de dezenas de repetições. As missões seguem a mesma linha: são genéricas, baseadas em matar X inimigos ou coletar X itens, sem impacto real no mundo. Para piorar, completar 100% das conquistas exige jogar um modo extra que vem via DLC pago e que, apesar de adicionar um pouco de lore, é chato e difícil.  [b]Considerações finais:[/b] Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night é um bom jogo, feito com carinho, identidade e respeito ao gênero. Ele acerta na ambientação, na trilha sonora, na variedade de conteúdo, na sensação clássica de exploração e na quantidade absurda de opções de customização. Ao mesmo tempo, tropeça em decisões de design que cansam, como menus pouco práticos, mapa limitado, sistema de morte cansativo, grind excessivo, chefes mecanicamente fracos e um final arrastado, com repetições de inimigos com pequenas alterações. É um jogo que diverte, agrada fãs de metroidvania e entrega uma experiência bacana, mas que constantemente dá a sensação de que poderia ser melhor com ajustes simples. Exactly what I wanted from a modern Castlevania SOTN style adventure. Exploring this castle was a marvelous time, with great music, strong atmosphere, and a satisfying flow through distinct areas like sewers, cathedral spaces, caves, underground labs, and deeper industrial zones. It also nails that classic Castlevania SOTN twist where you think you are done, but you are not, and the game rewards real exploration.  The best part is the gameplay freedom. Shards, weapons, and abilities let you experiment without forcing you to overthink it. Once I found a style I liked, it carried me comfortably to the end, and the boss fights were a highlight. Tough at first, then satisfying once you learn patterns and start treating them like a sparring match.  My main complaints are quality of life and signposting. The manual save room system is old school, and losing progress after a long stretch felt bad. I also hit a couple progression gates that were too opaque, including a key spike related armor upgrade and the way swimming unlocks, which pushed me to look things up. Still, I recommend it. The core experience is strong.  It's great Castlevania style castle exploration with great music and flexible builds!

Avec Bloodstained : Ritual of the Night (2019), c’est une envie de jeu de plateforme-action en 2D que je voulais assouvir. Après le semi-fiasco de la compilation Mega Man Legacy Collection (2015) qui m’a fait davantage souffrir que plaisir, j’ai jeté mon dévolu sur cette nouvelle franchise réalisée par un développeur de la licence phare Castlevania, à la grande époque de Konami. J’hésitais entre ce Bloodstained et mes compilations Castlevania achetées sur Nintendo Switch (Anniversary Collection et Dominus Collection). Bon, je crois que j’aurais dû choisir l’original à la copie, tout simplement. Non pas que la production de Koji Igarashi soit mauvaise, loin de là, mais elle se parcourt un peu sans saveur. Bloodstained n’a aucun défaut majeur selon moi, il manque juste de personnalité et d’une étincelle permettant d’accrocher pleinement au titre. Déjà, la direction artistique est vraiment passable pour ne pas dire décevante. Pour un jeu s’inscrivant dans les pas de la licence phare de Konami, c’est-à-dire un jeu à l’ambiance gothique et fantastique, nous sommes en droit d’attendre plus de soin apporté aux décors, aux personnages mais surtout aux ennemis. Les fautes de goût sur le design des ennemis sont trop nombreuses. Idem, l’apparence des PNJ est douteuse, les boss sont moches et les différents environnements sont au mieux correct au pire sans intérêt. Première douche froide sur l’ambiance visuelle générale qui se dégage du titre.  La seconde douche froide provient du scénario avec un personnage principal Miriam sans charisme, une histoire de cristaux et de possession à la con avec son ancien ami Gebel. Franchement, désolé, mais je n’ai rien compris et j’en n’avais rien à branler pendant les 23h qu’a duré ma partie. Encore une fois, c’est dommage. Ils ne peuvent pas faire des histoires plus simples, moins tarabiscotées, plus compréhensibles, moins connes ? Je ne sais pas, une histoire d’amour entre Miriam et Gebel, avec un peu de vengeance par ci, une trahison par là. Bon, pour la trahison, j’ai eu ma dose dans Bloodstained, c’est déjà ça.   Côté gameplay, Bloodstained a quand même un truc qui fonctionne, parce qu’il est réellement addictif. Le château est vaste, généreux, et on prend plaisir à retourner dans chaque zone, à explorer la carte jusqu’à la dernière pièce, puis à revenir plus tard avec un nouveau pouvoir, juste pour ouvrir une porte qu’on avait laissée derrière soi. On ressent aussi une vraie récompense liée au butin, avec beaucoup d’armes, beaucoup d’équipements, des trouvailles régulières, et un système de stats façon RPG plutôt agréable, qui donne l’impression de monter en puissance. Mais cette profusion a son revers, car la variété est en partie illusoire. L’efficacité, la rentabilité statistiques finit par tout écraser. Dans les faits, on garde souvent une poignée d’armes et on ignore le reste. Dès qu’une option est objectivement supérieure, le jeu pousse mécaniquement à la choisir. Résultat, une quantité d’objets reste au fond de l’inventaire et ne sont jamais utilisés. Pas de commentaire sur les quêtes annexes, la plupart sont des quêtes FedEx sans âme (paradoxalement) et le système de fabrication que ce soit pour les recettes (nourriture, armes, etc.) ou pour l’amélioration de ces pouvoirs n’est jamais vraiment très clair selon moi. On ne sait jamais vraiment si cela a un intérêt d’augmenter certaines capacités plutôt que d’autres, on tâtonne au pif.  N’étant pas un expert, j’éviterais les grandes envolées lyriques pleines d’emphases sur l’état des Castlevania « modernes » au regard du passé glorieux du genre. Je n’ai simplement pas été conquis par la proposition de 505 Games. Encore une fois, Bloodstained : Ritual of the Night reste un bon jeu vidéo mais l’expérience m’a juste paru un peu fade, faible esthétiquement et je ne pense pas en garder un souvenir impérissable. Le titre s’adresse principalement aux fameux « experts » du genre qui ont saigné tous les Castlevania et cherchent une alternative. Pour les néophytes comme moi, je pense que retourner en Transylvanie, au pays de Dracula, sera une meilleure idée même si le prochain Bloodstained : The Scarlet Engagement, annoncé pour 2026, semble prometteur. A voir…

이 게임을 메트로배니아를 좋아하는 분이 하시면 안됩니다. 절대 실망하실겁니다. 그 이유를 하나하나 말씀드리겠습니다.    기본적으로 메트로배니아의 장르라고 할 수 없는 요소가 메인으로 자리잡습니다. 1. 적들이 맵을 횡단하는 것만으로도 리스폰됩니다.       메트로배니아는 맵 탐사가 기본입니다. 적들과의 전투를 최소화 하는 대신, 맵을 꼼꼼히 살피지 못해 아이템도 챙기지 못하고 엄한데 깊게 들어가다 죽을것인가. 여러 리스크를 안고 적들을 소탕하여, 제대로 맵을 꼼꼼히 살피며 알뜰히 챙길것인가. 이 정도 선택권이 주워집니다. 헌데 이 게임은 소탕하여도 '아 막다른 길이네. 탐사 끝났고 아이템도 다 챙겼으니 다음 길을 찾아봐야지' 하는 순간 또 다시 소탕해야하는 상황이 생겨버립니다. 결국 전투도 최소화, 맵탐사도 최소화, 아이템도 챙길것만 빠르게 챙기고 돌아오는 선택밖에 주워지지 않습니다. 이건 뭐 스피드런 권장 게임입니까? 2. 저장하지 않는 이상 진행도는 저장되지 않습니다.       이 말이 무슨 말이냐. 위에서 말씀드린데로 메트로배니아는 맵 탐사가 메인 재미입니다. 내가 어디서 죽더라도, 어디까지 탐사했는지 기록이 남고, 진행하며 얻은 스킬이나 아이템 정도는 보장해줍니다. 그래야 내가 죽어도 맵을 탐사한 동기부여가 확실하게 남기 때문입니다. 내가 죽었을때 진행도가 전혀 남지 않는다면, 플레이어는 맵 탐사를 매우 소극적으로 하게 됩니다. 위 항목과의 시너지로 인해, 전투를 피하게 되고, 맵을 밝혀놔도 세이브까지 가지 않는 이상 다 부질없다는 생각이 남게됩니다. 3. 장비와 무기와 스킬 등의 호불호 요소 존재.       메트로배니아는 이미 정해진 무기와 장비로 얼마나 숙련도를 높이냐의 게임입니다. 단조로움을 피하기 위해 여러 다양한 종류가 추가되는 추세지만, 그 수가 많진 않으며, 장단점이 있고, 각 차이점이 뚜렷다하는 특징이 있습니다. 하지만 이 게임은 스킬요소나 무기등과 더불어, 장비와 그에 해당하는 스탯이 너무 많습니다. 메트로배니아보다 RPG게임에 훨씬 가깝습니다. 4. 회복약의 부재      회복약 자체는 존재합니다. 다만 일반적인 메트로배니아는, 기본적으로 주어지는 물약이 존재합니다. 그 기본물약의 횟수나 효율등을 높이는게 맵 탐사의 한 동기로 작동하기도 하며, 어느정도 리스크를 안고 스킬을 통해 추가적인 회복 수단을 가져오기도 합니다. 그러나 이 게임은 회복약을 소모품으로 채용했습니다. 늘 탐사를 나가기 전에 구매하거나 조재하여 충분한 물약을 지니고 출발해야 합니다. 더불어, 물약을 제한하지 않고 소모품으로 끝없이 가져갈 수 있게 되어, 맵탐사와 적소탕의 리스크라는 요소가 상당히 애매해졌습니다. 이 게임은 너무나도 본인이 RPG라고 주장하는 듯합니다.    전 이 게임이 당최 무슨 게임인지 모르겠습니다. 메트로배니아라고 하기엔 절대 미흡하고, RPG라고 하기엔 플레이 방식이 너무 어색하게 다가옵니다. 그래픽이나 모션도 좀 엉성한게 B급 감성이고. 상당히 실망했습니다.           ....뭐 물론 배에서 내리기 직전 보스는 끝내줬습니다. 근데 그게 답니다.    ...네. 다에요.

8 - Obra de arte Eu joguei CSOTN quando eu tinha 13 anos eu acho, então muita coisa eu não lembro, mas tem coisas que te marcam, a pixel ART, músicas, ambientação obviamente, mas cara até hj eu não achei um jogo que você se sinta da mesma forma que controlar o alucard, a coisa mais foda que tem é você dar aquela deslizada quando ele tá mudando de lado e METER UMA PORRADA NERVOSA no inimigo, você sente que o alucard tá colocando uma força disporporcional naquele golpe ele quer DIZIMAR o coitado do anão corcunda que tá na sua frente kkkkkk, dito isso, ainda sim eu tive uma relação ambígua com esse jogo na época, pq infelizmente o gênero metroidvania para mim complicado, ele tem muitas coisas que são qualidades para uns e defeitos para mim, um exemplo, é você ficar perdido caminhando a esmo em um lugar enorme, demorando horas de um lado a outro do mapa ate achar o objetivo, é uma das coisas mais insuportáveis para mim, porém, quando você tá perdido com o alucard, além de estar macetando os bixos na porrada e ouvindo as músicas igual uma rave no seu quarto, você dropa item, ganha level, quebra vela e isso te dá uma distraida do fato de você poder estar fazendo algo útil da sua vida, mas você tá perdido a 8h em um jogo, você não está só perdendo tempo, olha só, você está ganhando coisinhas,  só que isso não se repetiu nos metroidvanias inspirados em CSOTN, eu não entendo essa decisão, para mim isso é literalmente oq me fez zerar o jogo, sem essas coisas, eu ia ficar puto e ia dropar o jogo na primeira vez perdido, pq eu ia estar só perdendo tempo igual um otario e eu disse tudo isso, para dizer que bloodstained embora não tenha a sensação de controlar o alucard que é tão boa, para mim ele é uma evolução do sistema de exploraçao e combate do antigo, ele mitiga mais ainda a sensação de ser otario perdido, trazendo de volta o levelup, dinheiro, drop de itens e principalmente drop das magias dos inimigos que depende de você matar várias vezes eles, então se perder nesse jogo não é tão ruim, você acaba matando inimigos que te dão magias únicas, isso é simples mas é genial, em nenhum momento você se sente mal jogando, uma pena que a animação, personagens e história desse jogo sejam deprimentes, sério esse 3d é brochante por si só, mas comparado com a pixel ART do CSOTN fica piro ainda, os personagens não são carismáticos, a protagonista é uma PORTA, o vilão... Porta não dá para dizer que ela é jdjdkdkdkdk, é um PORTAO djjdjdkdk, mas é muito fraco sério e a história.... Era melhor ter ido ver o filme do pele, dito isso jogão, mas poderia ter sido muito melhor sem esses 3 defeitos.

[h3]这不是恶魔城的精神续作，这就是恶魔城！[/h3] （本测评为本体全成就测评，未游玩多周目多角色与dlc内容） 古老的僵硬手感，动听的哥特音乐，强大的人物性能，优秀的地图设计，丰富的游戏内容，狂刷不止的掉落素材，这就是最正宗的恶魔城风味，吸引人在城中探索，乐此不疲。唯一美中不足之处在于本作做了一些额外系统，像是任务系统、种植系统，但这些系统做的内容丰富度不够，像是本来想要做但是因为缺乏资金无法填充内容，仅仅保留了一个系统框架。 如果你是银河城游戏的爱好者，尤其是喜欢偏恶魔城方向设计的游戏，那一定不能错过本作。虽然本作原价入手也非常值得，但是505发行的游戏会频繁打折，很容易就可以遇到低价购买的机会。

令人着迷的精神续作，恶魔城粉丝的盛宴！  通关留念。这无疑是一款杰出的游戏，对于热爱《恶魔城》系列的玩家来说，它完美继承了经典的精髓，堪称一次令人满意甚至惊喜的体验。  ◆ 核心魅力：深度的复古操作  游戏最有趣的地方在于，它的一部分操作逻辑相当“老旧”，但这恰恰构成了其独特的深度和魅力。例如：  前期利用后撤步与下蹲相互取消来实现鬼畜式的快速移动，比走路快多了。  通过攻击落地取消、后撤步取消等技巧，可以打出“三联击”这类进阶操作。  这些设定让操作上限很高，喜欢钻研的玩家会乐在其中。  ◆ 难度与成长：舒适而充实  游戏难度曲线设计得很好，并没有刻意卡人。只要愿意适当刷刷级、培养角色，并囤积一些食物补给，通关过程会比较顺畅，兼具挑战与成长的正反馈。  ◆ 主要遗憾：两点不足  技能搓招：部分技能需要像格斗游戏一样搓招（如↓↘→+攻击），在快节奏战斗中偶尔会失误，如果能提供简化按键的选项会友好很多。  后期内容稍显仓促：个人感觉游戏后期节奏有点赶。尤其是刚拿到“瞬移”这类关键技能后，本以为会有一张全新地图去探索，没想到不久便迎来结局。考虑到这是众筹作品，可以理解，也让我更加期待今年即将推出的第二部能扩展更多内容！  ◆ 总结  瑕不掩瑜。它扎实的关卡设计、浓厚的恶魔城味道以及值得挖掘的操作系统，都让我乐在其中。如果你怀念那种探索、成长、战斗的经典银河城乐趣，这款游戏绝对值得入手。

[h1][b]Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night[/b][/h1]  [h3][b]Time Played:[/b][/h3]  64.1 Hours  [h3][b]Last Played:[/b][/h3]  23rd of July, 2021  [hr][/hr]  First having learned of this while it was still on Kickstarter, and relishing in it objectively showing just how completely disconnected both from the game market, and their own fans early-2010s Konami were, I had high hopes.  On playing it, I can say for a fact that while I did not find it quite as magical to me as my first playthtrough of Castlevania: Symphony of the night, I still find this series a worthy successor to what it was trying to replace  [h3][b]Yay:[/b][/h3]  + Tonnes of weapons  + Tonnes of subweapons/souls/shards  + Almost as many enemies, too...  + A massive array of armours, and other items.  + They expanded upon the in-Castlevania crafting systems quite well, in my eyes.  + Huge map...  + ...full of creative reinventions of many archetypes found in the Castlevania...  + ...and even some brand new ones.  + The library area, (which I loved, as I thought it reinvented the archetype) functions as a library for the first time in any “Igavania”  + [Spoiler]Hidden Desert, one of the most delightful, surprisingly-connected areas in any “metroidvania” I've ever played.[/spoiler]  +  Where I would usually get phantom sensation from my tenure in Arizona on merely seeing a “hot” area, the relevant archetype was one of my favourite areas in-game.  + [Spoiler]Den of Behehmoths spins a known area into something new and interesting.[/spoiler]  + Many, many, many hidden things, be these secret rooms, areas, an enemy, and yes, even a boss battle.  + Appearance customisation.  + Very creative bosses...  + ...and progression abilities.  + Vast array of cultures/mythologies represented.  + Returning bosses from Curse of the Moon have new moves, thus making the fights different.  + Multiple endings, and areas only able to be accessed after doing something specific related to one.  + Unlike Dawn of Sorrow, and Portrait of Ruin, they don't shove how to get the good end down your throat, if I'm remembering correctly.  + [Spoiler]That plot twist.[/Spoiler]  + Multiple playable characters, and extra stuff.  [h3][b]Meh:[/b][/h3]  ~ [Spoiler]Rather than an entire second castle, only 2 other areas open up after setting up the good ending. I loved said areas, though.[/Spoiler]  ~ In spite of the soundtrack being composed by none other than Michiru Yamane, I cannot remember a single song from the game, making it a massive disappointment in that department. I remember liking the Garden-type area's music, though, even if its melody cannot be recalled.  ~ The Classic 2 DLC added more achievements, and locked them behind a paywall.  [h3][b]Nay:[/b][/h3]  - Not only do they lock them behind DLC, but the Whip Sword, and Iga boss have no achievement associated with them, making the pack only for completion if you previously All-achievemented/Platinumed/Perfected the game/  - If you don't do the DLC on a fresh save, as well, the boss will likely go down in seconds, and item given, be useless.  [hr][/hr]  [h3][b]Verdict:[/b][/h3]  While the wait was long, I deem it to absolutely be worth it.   This game made me enjoy area archetypes I didn't in the past, threw some deeply-treasured new ones in there for good measure, then gave an open, ability-gated map platform RPG that's only slightly below what I remember from Symphony of the Night.  Thus, I very much await The Scarlet Encounter.  [h3][b]Placed in:[/b][/h3]  1: Fantastically-Favoured.

So I initially played this via GOG galaxy a few months after launch.  And whew, it took a while, I was taking my sweet time admittedly, but after clearing a playthrough I can definitely say it has changed notably but the foundation was all there already.  You get some costumes right off the bat, and my favorite build was nerfed *but* when its maxed out again the damage and such is still ok.  This game is an exciting platformer where a superhero demonstrates ridiculously powerful and varied abilities as she kicks ass across a doomed ship, a ruined town, and a massive fortress full of various rooms beyond.  The sheer variety in this game is impressive, though yeah sure games like Vampire Survivors have managed to top it.  I really appreciate that recolors aren't extremely common in this game like in other similar games (or RPGs in general), if nothing else the game commits to a decent amount of visual design.  Oh the recolors do show up later on, they even hand over different shards and drop different things like recolors traditionally do, but at least they aren't overwhelmingly common the way they are in a lot of other RPGs.  The music is great.  But it seems like a law where music in video games must be at least good if not amazing.  I still listen to some of the songs years after I finished my first run.  Yeah sure some of the enemy's death/defeat sounds are goofy, but the music and visual variety and weapon/gear variety is top notch.  You can kick enemies to death with ice skates, summon actual 8 bit monsters as backup, and block incoming attacks with (albeit nerfed) painting shield.  That's how silly this game can get.  I do hear that bugs show up from time to time, lag and poor reaction to controls and all that.  I haven't really experienced that myself, but even recently I talked to a friend about this game (thus playing it again) and yeah they brought it up too.  If that happens to you, my condolences.  I know how frustrating it is to find help for troubleshooting problems when others deny the problem exists and act like you're hallucinating, but honestly I haven't experienced those issues either.  All in all this was one of the best games of 2019 for me.  I can only hope you'll enjoy it too.  I really enjoyed my second run and a lot of the secret rooms were surprisingly unintuitive, like any 'proper' metroidvania's would be.  I'm sure you'd have a blast navigating this vast complex as I have!

They expect the player to grind grind grind. Repeat, repeat repeat. No thanks.

Bin etwas unzufrieden was Bloodstained:RotN angeht, ich habe nach ein weiteres gutes Metroidvania-Spiel gesucht welches an Blasphemous oder Hollow Knight rankommt, aber das war ein Griff ins Klo. Es gibt gewisse positive Dinge im Spiel, wie die sogenannten Shards oder etliche verschiedene Waffen die man aufm Weg findet, die recht innovativ sind und für Abwechslung im Kampf sorgen. Es wurden auch gewisse RPG-Elemente eingefügt die man sonst bei Metroidvania's nicht findet, wie zB ein Level-System zum Aufsteigen oder Rüstungen die bestimmte Attribute verstärken. Aber abgesehen davon wirkt dieses Spiel zu "künstlich", die unreal Engine Grafik wirkt atmosphärisch betrachtet eher kontraproduktiv. Its aight.  I've not gone too far into the Metroidvania genre, but I've dabbled, and I quite enjoyed Symphony of the Night. I think this is a perfectly enjoyable but unpolished experience.  + Certainly scratches that Castlevania SotN itch aesthetically and worldbuilding wise. I think the character designs are particularly interesting + Plenty of interesting enemies and bossfights + A solid soundtrack and excellent voice acting (I mean David Hayter is always going to be a win).  - Once you start seeing character models, textures or cutscenes up close, you can see the budget start to creep through. - A couple of bugs that aren't game breaking, but getting stuck on terrain could end up killing you if you aren't fast enough react. - I didn't find the story the most interesting thing in the world. Perfectly serviceable, but it just didn't connect.  Overall, I would recommend if you have a free backlog and its on sale.

The Castlevania reboot of your dreams.  Bloodstainde: Ritual of the Night is everything Mighty Number 9 wanted to be.  This is a true blue castlevania game, but with a whole new universe to explore and learn about.  This is made by a famous creator who was instrumental in creating the original series this is based on, and this game lives up to all the promises that were made during the kickstarter campaign.  Now, taking the history of this game out of the equation, this is still a fantastic Metroidvania with fun writing, cool levels and map design, awesome abilities, and great controls.  On top of the usual metroidvania abilities and platforming, this game also includes a secondary ability system and an armor/weapon system that's combined with a cosmetics system, so everything you equip can also be seen on your character.  The secondary ability system is similar to the ability system in Castlevania: Aria of Sorrow, where when you kill an enemy, there is a chance you will obtain an equipable ability from them.  This has its pros and cons, as it's great there is so much content, but if you want something particular or to complete everything, you're in for a lot of grinding.  On top of the great main campaign, there are also additional modes to play.  Boss rush, speed-run, and a second campaign where you play as a boss going through the whole game.  There is a LOT of content in this game.  While this IS a great game, it isn't perfect.  The game is obviously lower budget as it doesn't look quite as nice as it could, even for when it came out.  On top of that, the game screen can be a little difficult to read the screen because the foreground (what you can use) and the background (just visuals) are sometimes not as distinct as they could be.  These shouldn't really detract from how great this game is, but every game has it's flaws.  If you want another great metroidvania to play, this is a GREAT option, and the newly announced sequel (as of this writing) it's an even better time to jump on this game.

Shortly not a game for me as i like my Metroidvania games simple and usually in some retro visual-format but i am convinced by the sheer ambition seen here. 'A collectors dream for people who strive for that 100%' is a phrase i can believe in. They really threw in the sink and all here.   I am still early game. Probably because the game just throws endless stuff: craft items, new abilities right when you are still just learning the ropes and to me that is so distracting.  Constantly changing abilities and messing up with controls like a real newbie. Nice.  I could not do that but...I do. There is even mastery levels for different weapons and special moves for them. So much to take in but you don't need to understand it all. But apparently for many getting deep into all that is the juice that they squeeze from the game and it seems to be worth it for so many.   Now some real cons: Easy going mostly but some local areas where difficulty spikes and healing early on isn't that easy to come by. Combine that with the biggest fault this far: Floaty main character and inaccurate Gamepad controls making me experiment with keyboard to get it 'just right enough'  I really need to just let go of the stick when i strike as doing that stupid ground slide when fighting a boss is just too much.   Well anyways to conclude: I'd recommend putting a wee bit more time into looking into this via gameplay videos. I do understand the negatives here too but i still dont want to trash this magnificent 'big thing'  even if it's obvious this is not my tea and likely will not ever complete this one but it's entertaining still. If nothing else collecting those xp levels and trying out weapon combinations do bring some satisfaction to me. And the exploration isn't that bad so do take a closer look.
