Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night — When Nostalgia Bites Back  Let’s get this out of the way early: I’m not a Metroidvania person. The genre and I have an understanding — I don’t ask it to respect my time, and it doesn’t ask me to enjoy backtracking. That said, Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night didn’t actively repel me, which already puts it ahead of several of its peers.   Visually, though? That’s where things get… complicated. I both liked and disliked the art direction simultaneously. The aesthetic is chaotic in a way that’s occasionally charming but mostly incoherent. Enemy designs are all over the place — bees, floating terrier dog heads on chains, frogs, armored knights, sexy demon women — it feels less like a carefully constructed gothic world and more like someone dumped an entire sketchbook into the engine and said, “Yeah, all of that goes in.”  It’s funny to look at, sure, but it nukes any sense of worldbuilding. Nothing feels unified. It’s like a kindergartener sneezed ideas onto construction paper and the devs said, “Perfect. Ship it.”  Combat doesn’t help much either.  Combat is extremely basic. Swing weapon. Step away. Swing again. Repeat until the enemy politely stops existing. There are Shards — demonic abilities you absorb from enemies — and some of them are genuinely interesting, but they never quite elevate the combat beyond functional.  It plays like an early 2000s MMO. You left-click until the thing dies, and if you’re conscious enough, you dodge. Riveting stuff.  Which is frustrating, because the systems suggest depth. Weapon variety, shard combinations, passive buffs — the bones are there, but the execution rarely demands creativity unless you’re deliberately experimenting.  The story doesn’t exactly save it.   There’s betrayal. There’s a castle. There’s a samurai. He has a sister. You meet them briefly. And that’s… kind of it.  The game never takes the time to flesh out its characters or lore in a meaningful way. Miriam is fine as a protagonist, but emotionally distant, and everyone else feels like they wandered in from a different draft of the script.  So I didn’t care. About anyone. Or anything. At all.  I reached the normal ending, where you rush Gebel as early as possible.  Apparently there’s a true ending that requires running around the castle like a Roomba with anxiety, collecting specific items and checking invisible boxes. I didn’t bother.  I didn’t like the game enough to justify Olympic-level hoop jumping.  Traversal doesn’t help. Fast travel exists, but only via mirrors that are scattered across the map like someone actively tried to inconvenience you. They’re spaced far enough apart that backtracking becomes a chore rather than a strategic choice.  Now, to be fair…  If what you want is an old-school action-exploration game in the vein of Castlevania: Symphony of the Night, this is about as close as you’re going to get without legally summoning the year 1997.  It’s not just inspired by Symphony of the Night — it practically introduces itself by saying, “Hello, I am Symphony of the Night’s legally distinct cousin.”  Castle to explore? Check. Monsters to slay? Check. Weapons and powers everywhere? Check.  And Koji Igarashi — the man himself — hasn’t lost the juice. The customization is genuinely impressive. There are tons of builds you can run, mixing weapons, shards, and passive abilities in ways that allow real player expression.  But then the problems stack up again.  This game is anchored to the past. So anchored it’s practically underwater. It doesn’t add much of anything new to the Metroidvania genre — especially ironic given how much Igarashi himself contributed to shaping it in the first place.  Combat suffers from hitbox issues. Enemy behavior can be erratic. There are script hiccups. It’s not broken, but it’s rough around the edges in ways that feel avoidable.   And grinding. Oh my god, the grinding.  Shards are luck-based. You might grind an enemy for ages just to get an ability that isn’t even that good. It does allow you to define your own playstyle, but the cost is tedium.  Map design is another sticking point.  Areas blend together visually. It’s difficult to distinguish zones at a glance. Color-coding or clearer labeling would have helped enormously.  Instead, everything looks like the same gothic hallway wearing a slightly different hat.  Graphics don’t impress either. The 3D visuals look cheap, especially compared to how stunning a well-crafted 2D art style could’ve been. Add in clunky inverted ability controls with no satisfying keybind options, and it’s death by a thousand small irritations.  Still…  Despite all of that, it is a solid spiritual successor. If you’re a Castlevania fan, there’s a lot here to like.  Just don’t expect it to revolutionize anything.  Voice One: There are simply too many better Metroidvanias on the market now that do the same thing — but tighter, cleaner, and with fewer nostalgia blinders.  ✅ 5 Good Points  Strong spiritual successor to Symphony of the Night  Deep customization with weapons, shards, and builds  Large castle with plenty to explore  Faithful old-school Metroidvania feel  Solid foundation for Castlevania fans  ❌ 5 Bad Points  Incoherent visual identity hurts worldbuilding  Combat is basic and lacks evolution  Poor map readability and tedious traversal  Luck-based shard grinding  Too derivative, adds little to the genre  Final Verdict  I’m not sure I’d recommend this to anyone unless you specifically crave a basic, old-school Metroidvania or are deeply nostalgic for Symphony of the Night. As a tribute, it works. As a modern evolution? Not so much.  Score: 7 out of 10  A respectable effort, occasionally charming, often frustrating — and ultimately overshadowed by better alternatives that learned from the past instead of living in it.

相比其他类银，有五十岚孝司参与制作的赤痕夜之仪式更偏恶魔城。 本作有一套rpg系统，玩家有各项属性值和等级成长，同时还包括大量的装备以及技能(即游戏中的碎片)。武器种类多，还有各种不同效果的配件等。玩家还有一个村庄据点，有商店和合成以及NPC任务。为了更好地提升能力，玩家不仅仅要积极探索地图，还需要刷材料、刷钱等。战斗中玩家也能直接开菜单使用道具等等。本作同样也有能力锁相关设计，但相对而言比较弱化。一些特殊能力比如让场景颠倒之类的和其他类银设计的能力还是差别比较大的。  本作地图规模比较大，战斗占比也比较高，基本上没有什么解谜。不过主线指引比较弱，不会直接标记下一个地点。剧情而言是经典的恶魔和城堡题材，不过打通玩下来而言，其实比较薄弱的，算是比较简单的剧情，笔墨较少。包括NPC任务也没什么文本，就是刷怪和给东西。画面虽然有采用3D建模，不过视角仍然是平面横版，同时有些简陋。  喜欢类银的玩家需要注意其RPG玩法系统，能接受刷刷刷和大量战斗的可以试试。

玩起来还行，但是关卡谜题设计很垃圾，目前还没打完，记录一下我卡关的地方： 1.坐火车需要一个道具，然而没有任何指引告诉玩家这个道具如何获得。首先需要得到一个道具来激活照相机，在哪得到的我已经不记得了，照相机在商店左边的尽头，我去照相机那里瞎按的时候发现不知道啥时候得到了这个激活照相的道具。然后，拿到相片还没结束，还需要去图书馆得到证件，得到证件后才能坐火车。哪个天才想出的谜题，相片、图书馆，要不是查了攻略，谁能把这些东西跟火车联系起来？既然难以联想到一起，给个主线任务列表，在任务详情里直接写给玩家看好不好？ 2.拿了吸血后，探图半天，很确定已经没有现在能去的地方了（除了一个8bit的隐藏房间，那地方怪40级，还堆怪，我才26级，那很明显了这地方不是现在该去的。另一个还能去的地方是boss，打完就gameover），又卡关了，查半天攻略，原来潜水需要打一个水母怪爆它的碎片魔法。这种探图关键道具竟然是打怪掉的，还是概率掉不是必掉，又是哪个天才想出来的？怎么光线、二段跳这些能力都知道放个boss上给玩家必掉，到了潜水这就得盯着那一个小怪可劲薅等他随机爆？况且我角色在还不会潜水的时候在水中作战本身就是劣势，一般来讲为了避开劣势基本都是赶紧赶路避战吧，就算凑图鉴那也是杀一个水母得了，不看攻略的话，我是真拿不到这个碎片魔法，就算运气好拿到了，也未必想得到这个打怪随机爆的魔法是探图必要条件。  目前就打到这里，本来战斗上经常跟敌人站太近导致连续卡碰撞体连续被判受伤已经很糟糕了，但银河城的底子在这，探索还算有趣，但是这几个谜题真是神人才能设计出来。密特罗德的含金量进一步上升了。 另外，查攻略时还不经意看到了评论老哥说地图中一篇刺刺的通道那里是需要穿一件防刺装备来过的，我本来以为要用一个空中冲刺一类的能力来过，我要是没看到这评论，这地方也妥妥的要卡关了。这件装备我还没拿到，但跟水母爆潜水碎片魔法一样，我真的不理解什么神人策划想得出来 把这种 在其他游戏里都要生怕玩家找不到的重要功能 放在一个小怪随机爆的道具上，还不给点提示。是怕我找得到吗？ 噢还有说到这我想起来了，游戏早期教的那个，用火魔法点大炮，本来觉得挺有意思的，结果后面完全就没有了  通关后补充： 战斗方面你将体验：碰撞怪物后，双方不弹开，而是反复触发碰撞伤害；飞行怪物除了飞过来打你以外的时间大多在与你拉开距离，并且穿到墙后面甚至穿到画面外，单方面攻击玩家；有些boss还好，摸清规律还能打过，有些boss我是拼尽全力无法战胜，是我比较菜，于是选择去刷怪练级，刷了10级还是打不过，对不起我太菜了，也不想有更多刷怪的无意义垃圾时间了，遂使用修改器过了那几个boss。所以说，手残真别来玩吧。 这游戏有很多早年老游戏的缺点，这游戏发售时已经2019了吧，想不到还会这样。上一个让我有这种感觉的是gamefreak，但是他们是技术力拉，这是设计拉

续作宣布26年了，终于来了，补个标。 6/10 即使是扔19年也不太能令人接受的画面…… 战斗系统相较刻印的创新来说总体显得保守了很多，瞬发法术和目标法术的分离很好但远远不够。 系列一贯而之的需要后退取消的“僵硬”手感尽管是特色且和飞行射击游戏做出了区分，但这种微妙的手感显然在3D画面中得到了放大。 总之毕竟是独立出来的小作坊作品，各种方面可以理解但达不到值得推荐的地步。

If you are a Castlevania fan, you will see all the ADN in here. While I may have some minor nitpicks here and there, I'd say for the most part you can see the evolution of the series through this game, and in my opinion, for the most part, you get the closest to a definitive version of those systems here. Overall, a really nice experience, though I'd recommend it more to Castlevania fans, I feel non-Castlevania fans would also get a lot out of it, after all, this is the result of various attempts at improving upon the formula.

跨别多年的一场等待，给恶魔城的一封情书 本来就是众筹粉丝向的作品没什么好说的。画面和艺术风格挺独特的，2.5D美术融合哥特式建筑、血肉诅咒与蒸汽朋克元素，场景细节丰富，当然放在2019年的话，部分特效还是挺廉价的。当初特别喜欢封面上女主的插画，胸前碎片人的纹饰很吸引我的眼球，但建模只能说差强人意，看久了还是不错的。 血污的配乐完全就是山根美智留的新巅峰，比之月下可谓有过之而无不及。刚进游戏界面就硬控了我整整两分钟，心情还未平复下来，Voyage of Promise和Luxurious Overture又将游戏的气氛引向高潮，后面Holy Wisdom、Gears of Fortune和Ritual of the Night仿佛是老阿姨的炫技表演，不由得让人回忆起曾经矩形波俱乐部的荣光，慨叹良多。 游戏的玩法和成长系统诚意很大、自由度很高。恶魔城独有的探索类型挺喜欢，虽然可收集的碎片和武器装备很多，但从我全收集的历程来看，很多碎片和武器并不那么好用，我用手柄搓招也有些狼狈，知道必杀技熟练度没有全熟练成就后也就果断放弃搓招了。剧情中规中矩。传统“复仇+救赎”剧本，角色台词偏日式王道风格，碎片化文档补充世界观。地图设计不错，立体式迷宫结合新碎片能力锁。记得有部分NPC和文本会提示过图路线和如何利用能力。纵向分层：通过“双塔钟楼——地下炼金工坊——屋顶花园”形成垂直空间嵌套，需反复利用新能力(如二段跳或反转)解锁路径。横向联通：隐藏墙壁与多向通道密集(如大教堂区域三条暗道交汇)。不同区域的主题也做到了差异化，哥特教堂(彩窗光影)、血肉实验室(蠕动器官)、蒸汽火车(动态场景)等。不过后期的地图很明显经费不足，设计的有点仓促了。

10/10 By One Who Walked the Corridors of Time  My whip once danced in Harmony of Dissonance, My soul was reaped in Dawn of Sorrow's trance. I've chased Circles of the Moon through cursed night, And sealed the Cross where Soma claimed his right. From Aria's melody to Ecclesia's seal, Now Bloodstained whispers: "The old magic is real."  They said the castle fell with Dracula's last breath, That Belmont's lineage slept in noble death, That Alucard's pallor faded with the years— But look! The gothic spires, the crystal tears, The shard-bound artistry of Miriam's hand Proves: The night still bleeds at IGA's command.  Oh, let the critics scoff at "retro" claims— I know these halls, these torches, these old flames. When Gebel's wings eclipse the fractured moon, I hear Soma's chaos sing the same dark tune. Each fragmented glyph, each alchemic brew Tastes of Aria's sorrow, Julius's virtue.  So raise your glass to portraits weeping blood, To stairs that coil where ancient demons stood. For we who mapped Castlevania's bones Find in this Ritual—our true home.

I don't know what to say about this one. Is it the best Metroidvania ever made? Is it worth whatever price you find it at? Am I eagerly awaiting The Scarlet Engagement?  The answer to all these questions is "Yes."  Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night carries on the legacy of the Castlevania games, and is clearly the culmination of many years of experience and expertise. The mastermind behind it knows what makes the genre work, and blends it all together in a package here that has more content than anyone knows what to do with. While my hours played is well over 200 (and those are real hours played, not a result of simply leaving the game open by mistake), it's because I speedrun the game, not because I've seen everything it has to offer. I've still barely touched Classic Mode or Classic Mode 2 (admittedly, the latter of which is DLC).   There are four characters with whom you can go through the normal game, each with their own unique abilities, similar to how previous Castlevania games would have an Albus Mode or Julius Mode. While these are a bit bare compared to Miriam Mode (the default/main character), each still provides the amount of content you'd get from any Metroidvania you'd pick up here on Steam.   It certainly isn't a coincidence that the subtitle for this game is only one word off from "Symphony of the Night." This game takes everything SOTN did, does it better, and then multiplies it by ten. There's a huge variety of weapons to choose from, though you'll probably make the mistake of sticking with the basic swords for your first run, as I did, neglecting some of the more amazing weapons, like the Scythe, or the simple Kung Fu Shoes, whose technique Flashing Air Kick allows some truly glorious sequence breaking. If Melee isn't your thing, that isn't a problem either, as every enemy in the game (except one, the basic Morté, what would be called a Zombie in Castlevania) has a Shard to drop, and every Shard allows Miriam to access a new ability, be it a fire spell, a summoned chainsaw, a familiar, or the ability to turn the entire world upside down--like the upside-down castle of Symphony of the Night, except you control it and can switch it on and off any time you want.   Truly a masterpiece.   Ritual of the Night nails the experience of starting the player weak and without much to do ability-wise, and then slowly progressing and powering up the player until you are a goddess rampaging through the castle and destroying everything in sight. Unusually, though, Miriam is not truly weak and powerless from the start, if you know how to use the abilities she begins the game with. While you may frown upon seeing the basic and seemingly-weak Kung Fu Shoes as the starting weapon, you should be aware that the Kung Fu Shoes are a primary staple of speedruns, and are far from useless, even if you aren't trying to reach places you shouldn't be able to reach yet.   You might think, with so many shards to be collected, that it's a grindfest, but this problem is expertly dodged by two facts. First is that every shard has a 4% base droprate, minus one or two outliers. This means that, at worst, an enemy may need to be killed 25 times before you get their shard, which is a huge improvement over some of the 0.5% droprates that some Metroidvanias have. However, the second stroke of genius is that Luck is your most useful stat, and there are ways to begin boosting it very early on. Luck has a direct and noticeable effect on drop rates for items and shards, and is the single most important stat to stack through the game. If you're anything like me, you'll reach an area full of new enemies and immediately feel the need to grind it out until you have the shard from every enemy. Unlike many games, though, it doesn't feel like a drag here, as you'll spend a few minutes doing that, and then you'll be ready to move on. You'll never spend 20 minutes grinding against a single enemy to get their shard, something I've done far too many times in Aria of Sorrow and Dawn of Sorrow.   It simply feels good to do this. While there are many shards of questionable utility (like Summon Toad), just as many are surprisingly useful, and the desire to know whether an enemy gives something good or something useless is easily enough to compel players to grind them all out. And, importantly, it never really feels like grinding, except on those rare movements that you find the Augment Luck shard and realize that it's in your best long-term interests to stop everything and grind out 9 of them to max out its potential. That can actually take a while, but usually it's Rank you'll want to increase, which is done somewhere else, and not Grade, which is increased by collecting duplicates of the shard.   That "other place," where you increase Rank, is with Johannes, who provides access to the crafting system, the cooking system, and the shard upgrade system. This is the only area where I've found any flaws in the game, as it's all a bit obtuse and unclear. It's mostly a UI problem, because some weapons require other weapons in order to be crafted, making some armor and weapon crafting like a tree, while everything is presented as a list. It isn't too much of an issue, but it is an issue, made worse by the fact that you'll also need to find recipes in the castle to make certain things.   Good luck trying to figure out why you can't craft a Tea Dress because it doesn't even show up on your list, making you think you need a recipe, when really it's just that you don't have any Durable Rags and you used the last Cotton you had making Bat Wings... Since you don't have any of the materials required, it simply won't show up on your list of craftable items. Like I said, it can be obtuse.   Graphically, the game is beautiful, and musically it has all the awesomeness you'd expect of a Castlevania game--because this is, in everything but name, the ultimate Castlevania.   I've played a lot of Metroidvanias. Gal Guardians, Ender Lilies, AfterImage, Blasphemous, Hollow Knight, Castlevania: Dominus Collection, I could go on. But there's no need. We can add a hundred more to that list, and the result would be the same--Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night is the best of them. It's no contest. If you like Metroidvanias, then you need to play this. It's the best.

There is an utterly horrendous grind that takes 5+ hours of solid backtracking and grinding to 100%, maybe more. I still haven't brought myself to do the grind. This game was a journey for me.   I'm a big fan of modern metroidvania's.  I grew up playing the classic ones and thought they were good at the time but never loved them as much as most people.  Symphony was a 7/10 to me back in the day...the DS games were slightly better depending on he game but typically below an 8.   Meanwhile I loved games like Ori, Guacamelee, PoP Lost Crown, The Messenger.   I finally got around to playing Bloodstained and the experience of the first third of this game was miserable after the intro sequence.  It was slow, poorly voiced, and came off as absurdly grindy with bad drop rates and really boring farming.  This never really goes away...the game was 'better than the sum of its parts' by just having a chill fun to it but it was also like a 5/10 during this early portion.   Eventually I got a feel for what NEEDED to be farmed and when I could just have fun with the game.  As you unlock more and more upgrades the game creates this really strong sense of progression and traversal upgrades.  I pretty much 100% this game when I was done with it and I never would have expected it.   I still don't rank it among the best in the genre as I suspect many do....but I'm settling on a 7/10 based on a first playthrough.  I'm excited by the other playable characters, a randomizer, and just trying the game again in the future understanding the systems and general path better.  I suspect this game will shine brighter on a second playthrough now that I won't be wasting so much time farming things I didn't need to originally and referencing as much stuff online.   I recommend the game.  Its possible on revisit this game goes from good to truly great as well which is extremely rare for me.  I enjoyed it enough that I'm now interested in the sequel next year.

I tried my best to like this game. After all, I'm usually fond of metroidvanias, and this one was made by Koji Igarashi, the creator of Symphony of the Night, which is one of the genre's greats. However, as I forced myself through the early parts of Bloodstained, I became more and more certain that Igarashi doesn't understand what made SoTN so beloved in the first place. They tried making the game great [i]because of[/i] things that SoTN was great [i]in spite of[/i].  First of all, the great-grandfather of all its sins, the one that made me put it down after not even 3 hours, unlikely to ever pick it up again - the controls. They are atrocious. Miriam controls somewhat like Alucard - as in, stiff, wooden and entirely unsuited for a 2D platformer wannabe. No momentum on the ground, no control in the air, awkward hitbox, no acceleration. In SoTN's case, I can grit my teeth, repeat "it's from 1997" until the mantra flows out of my ears, but the problem is, it doesn't fly 22 years later, not after 2 Ori games' and Hollow Knight's hummingbird-precision controls.  Second, not entirely unrelated, the difficulty curve. It's a given that with RPGs, the game is most difficult when it actually should be the easiest, near the beginning, as the player lacks both power and experience with the game.  A good RPG needs to address this. Bloodstains decides not to address it, and leaves players amongst a horde of enemies itching to devour your low-leveled protagonist. And when they do... back to the title screen with you. Along with potentially hours of progress. Because, unlike most modern games, where save points are on the main path in reasonable, regular intervals, Bloodstained chooses SoTN's 1997 way of having save rooms tucked away, unmarked and off the beaten path. Autosaves are nonexistant. If you die, it's not that you have to do a corpse run to recover XP or gold, but everything you've done since the last manual save goes down the drain. Again, this practice had its technical reasons in 1997, but there's no excuse in 2019.  So, long story short, avoid this exercise in frustration. If you want a good metroidvania, play Prince of Persia The Lost Crown, Ori, Hollow Knight, Blasphemous, Metroid Dread, AM2R, Ender Magnolia, Dead cells, Yoku's Island Express, Constance, literally anything else with the tag "Metroidvania".

It's a blatant ripoff of Castlevania: Symphony of the Night, but it's done so well I don't really mind. A little on the short side, you can probably 100% the base game mode in 30h (or less for speedrunners). Everything flows smoothly, and most progression is intuitive. It's a delight to play.  4/5 stars.

tl;dr: huid, ni Igarashi, ni heredero de Castlevania ni leches.  Dicen que es uno de los imprescindibles del género pero ha sido una decepción mayúscula. De primeras creía que era un juego de 2012, viendo su aspecto gráfico y mecánicas, hasta que comprobé que no, es de 2019 (como dato, dos años después de la salida de Hollow Knight).  Es un juego muy anticuado, parece más bien un remaster (cutre) de Symphony of the Night que un juego nuevo.  - La progresión no se guarda automáticamente, tienes que ir a un punto de guardado, y si mueres el mapa que hayas descubierto se pierde, así como los objetos que hayas recogido o los jefes que hayas matado, tienes que repetir todo. Es 2019, no me vale con decir que es un Castlevania puro, el autoguardado bien entendido es Quality of Life.  - Enemigos que reaparecen al volver a entrar a una sala, listos para grindear y romper el juego. La exploración se hace tediosa, y si le sumas lo que digo arriba más.  - La protagonista es muy lenta y el moveset es básico, saltar y poco más, ni un triste dash; bueno, tiene un esquive hacia atrás que para nostálgicos supongo que bien. Las habilidades de movimiento, a excepción del doble salto, no son excesivamente naturales de ejecutar, haciendo combos o usando un fragmento equipado.   - Diseño de niveles muy muy pobre, cero plataformeo (y digo cero de verdad), y áreas muy lineales, básicamente un pasillo con solo un camino correcto y todo lo demás son salas sin salidas con un cofre. Básicamente pegar a masillas, llegar al portal de teletransporte y el punto de guardado (al lado del jefe) y matar al jefe. Puzles, por supuesto, ninguno.  - Momentos de bloqueo artificiales, por ejemplo cuando para continuar por una zona de pinchos debes encontrar un objeto que no se referencia en ningún sitio, solo puedes encontrarlo a base de explorar todo el mapa. Esto es especialmente doloroso porque en la tienda los npc te dan alguna pista, pero de esto nada.  - Los sistemas en general son caóticos, que si fragmentos, habilidades, atajos, combos, crafteo, comidas... Tiene una cantidad de cosas innecesarias para una partida base que directamente lo ignoras o lo olvidas.  - Los enemigos son de lo peor que he visto en mucho tiempo. Aparte de no suponer ningún reto real (simplemente comen más o menos golpes), son rematadamente feos. Supongo que IGA pensó que un demonio infernal podía ser cualquier cosa pero poner la cabeza flotante de un perro pues mira, no. Los bosses igual, bizarros de más, algunos sin sentido en la historia, feos a rabiar y simplones.  - Fácil, a nada que te preocupes por subir de nivel las cosas con las que estás cómodo raro será el área que te cueste o el boss que se te resista, prácticamente todos los puedes matar aporreando sin más.  - Gráficamente es tremendamente feo, el 2.5D le va fatal, las texturas e iluminación son horribles, y en general es difícil distinguir el personaje del fondo según en qué áreas.  - Demasiado japonés para mi, con bien de gemidos orgásmiscos, personajes con tetazas, diálogos cansinos, historia basiquísima, previsible y repetitiva de otros Castlevania.  - La BSO tiene ese típico toque Castlevania que a veces parece la música de los coches de choque de la feria y luego te meten unos temazos heavy que para qué.  Vamos, que por si no ha quedado claro, me ha parecido una castaña tremenda, no se lo recomiendo a nadie.

Normal Maps - The Game™  I’m not sure whether this game was ever meant to be taken seriously, but it’s goofy on so many levels that I enjoyed it as a humorous take on a classic metroidvania. The art is inconsistent: some areas look nice with a decent mood and color choices, while others are a crazy mix of elements (and don’t forget the normal maps!). Overall it’s quite pleasant to look at for its release year. The characters are... there. The voice work is... interesting. When you first start, some of the item/skill/crafting menus can look overwhelming, but that eases with more playtime. If you give it time, it keeps getting better. You get neat weapons and abilities, reasonably challenging bosses, and some story as well.  My main gripe is some of the exploration. The story and voice work are so goofy most of the time that you don’t take them seriously, but there may be important hints there for progressing and getting the right ending. At one point, after exploring the map to nearly 100%, I looked at a guide and was glad I did. From there it was smooth sailing. I finished the game but still need to return to get a secret level I opened once, died in, and then forgot to finish.  Overall, it’s easily in the top quarter of games of this type, and if you understand what you’re getting into there’s plenty of fun to be had.

大前提として、良作です。その上で細かいところを説明してみます。  ゲームも優れていますが、BGMが大変すばらしいです。 イコライザなどをつかって自分好みに上手く調整すると、どれだけ優れているのか実感できると思います。  3Dになっている関係で当たり判定が若干わかりにくい部分があります。 大きな問題にはなりませんが、ドット単位での攻防を楽しむようなプレイは今作では難しい場面もあります。 逆に3Dを活かしたエリア構造も存在するので、良い部分も大きいです。  豊富なゲームモードがあり、おまけ要素に近いものから本格的なボリュームを伴ったものまで多種多様。 ランダマイザー（いくつかの任意の要素が無秩序に入れ替わる仕組みのゲームモード）も搭載されているので、その気になればずっと遊び続けられます。 ただし、よくランダマイザー系MOD動画で見るような『妙なところに強敵がいる』というランダム化は出来なくて、そこはちょっと自由度が低いかと感じました。 また、協力プレイとありますが、本編では残念ながらできません。一部のおまけモード専用です。 実は純粋なアクションゲームでステージクリア型のゲームモードも標準搭載されています。某シリーズファン必見。  月下の夜想曲をプレイしたことがある人ならわかるネタがあらゆる場所に散りばめられています。 徹底的といってもいいほどです。 さらにバッカー達による追加要素も山のようにあり、本作の一部は確実にファンの手で作られています。  ビジュアル面では若干のインディー感が否めない部分も存在します。遊んでいたら気にならなくなる範囲。  以上が正直な感想になります。 例のシリーズとは雰囲気が違うのも事実です。しかしこれも悪くないので、シリーズファンの方もぜひ。

真的是太好玩了，還記得第一次打開遊戲選擇了『斬月』，然後被血虐到懷疑人生，於是便按步就班的從米莉安開始進行冒險。 從一開始的慎重玩法，到後來的奔放掃圖，在這漫長的刷寶、刷結晶、刷經驗、刷錢，逐步強化的過程，從不會感覺到無聊，因為可以搭配的玩法非常多花樣，記得我兒子第一次打木匠的時候被虐很慘，木匠會一直靠近還時不時射飛刀，一下都一百多滴血，打了好多次都快崩潰了，於是我和兒子開始討論是否配招的問題，然後我發現有一招『定向盾牌』好像可以一戰，換上試用一下就直接上了，結果一次妥妥過，再之後打 IGA 也是靠定向盾牌過關的，這招實在太變態了。  我兒子破關後就不玩了，我繼續把自己的進度破完後接著挑戰斬月，花了四個多小時終於也破關了，感動到不行，實在是太好玩了，意猶未盡！！  對了，我和我兒子都是玩普通難度，惡夢不敢挑戰。  總之，非常推薦喜歡玩橫向捲軸動作冒險遊戲的玩家，必須購買並且玩它，保證會愛上的，而且音樂超超超級神！！！  好聽到爆！！

Отличная метроидвания от одного из создателей жанра — Кодзи Игараси. Чувствуется влияние "Симфонии ночи": те же геймплейные механики с большим упором на прокачку, огромное количество экипировки и косметики, что особенно радует, учитывая, что ГГ женского пола. Также доставляет удовольствие атмосфера всего происходящего. Достаточно продуманное строение уровней, классный дизайн монстров, интересная история мира и сюжет. Очень рекомендую эту "Игарасиванию", тем более на ПК нет конкурентов этой игре.

也是第一次玩比较纯正的恶魔城，刨开画风过于复古这一点外体验还不错，另外总感觉分辨率怪怪的？ 游戏存在大量的刷刷刷环节，练级刷钱刷碎片刷材料全都是不得不品的一环，当然不刷靠技术通关也是完全可行，但如果目标是全收集那么机械的重复劳作就必不可少了。刷成型后的主角性能过于夸张，过图都是飞打怪都是秒，但不刷就有点像蛆在蠕动，反差有点忒大了。 操作方面，没有冲刺会让很多没玩过恶魔城的玩家极其不习惯，战斗动作也略显僵硬，但我不死鸟都吃下去了还怕这个？此外虽然配套了许多武器许多动作模组许多搓招，但基本最后还是AAA，进阶一点就是后撤步或者小跳取消平A后摇，技能不要太难搓，我熟练度成就都是拿把刀找个傻大个格挡刷满的，我是真没招了。 美术方面，有点太廉价了，小怪塑料换皮，技能特效也略显敷衍，但我对这方面要求不高，雷画风的需着重考虑。 音乐方面，个人感觉音乐设计还算不赖，但部分地图的音乐过于突出了导致有些喧宾夺主，点名双龙之塔。 地图设计层次感不错，同为方格设计，个人认为地图设计比莉莉优秀，反转的能力设计也是令我眼前一亮，只是有些能力锁锁得我头有点大，拿了吸血我没想到吸血池；连续的荆棘地那一块我压根没想到是有一个防具能免疫荆棘，我还以为有超冲能力没拿到，洞批无言了；以及最后拿了斩月找月亮，压根记不得位置。 此外，这游戏BOSS前都有给椅子，希望广大类银河恶魔城游戏学习！！！！！！！！！！！！ 综合来看还是不错的，对画风和动作流畅性没有太高要求的话依然值得尝试，探图成长的正反馈还是很足的。   最后，全成就玩家千万别买，这沟槽游戏强行加了DLC成就，DLC更是一大坨，经典模式有点太经典了，画风更加复古手感更加稀碎，容错还比本体低，我是直接跟着视频一比一复刻跑了，即使这样也很折磨。

[i]I have not finished the game and unlikely to do so[/i] [b][i]Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night[/i][/b] is a Metroidvania. The protagonist, Miriam, awakens after a 10-year-long coma when her friend tries to open a portal for a demonic invasion.   The combination of the bosses missing health bars, the dodge being a backstep, every enemy causing contact damage and them reviving upon reentering rooms discourage me from continuing. Additionally, the game occasionally crashes and the special attacks do not seem to be adjusted for the keyboard.   There are crafting and a lot of weapons to choose from, but I have not engaged much with these systems. The protagonist gains XP from defeating opponents which I think linearly increases the stats. There are very few pieces of equipment that change the appearance, but the weapons do have different movesets.   There have been quite a lot of crossovers between [i]Bloodstained[/i] and other games, which does not help with the immersion either. The alternative main characters can run through the same levels, but without crafting or equipment or story (Aurora's plot is text-based only, but rhymes).   The 3D models and environments with the 2D gameplay look tacky and make it hard to see the platforms. The character design is poor, with the protagonist wearing a dress and armoured gauntlets at the same time. There are backers' portraits in many of the locations, which, while not unfitting in terms of story, have a drastically different style than any other 2D or 3D art.   The protagonist's screams on each executed attack are unpleasant to hear and the music is repetitive. The game is fully voiced and I wish it was not.   The controls are rebindable, but I could not switch aiming from the mouse nor the menu controls. The game uses manual saving only at checkpoints. There are several save slots.   Overall, I would recommend avoiding this.

9/10   惡魔城的精神續作，普通難度100%通關，遊戲還有其他內容如無血/斬月、Boss復仇等等，也有惡夢難度可選，非常好玩，通常這種能讓我一直不斷玩玩到底的遊戲都會給10/10，但這款的小毛病多到會笑出來。  進遊戲一陣子就會發現文本有瑕疵，名字不一樣、錯字、漏字、不同裝備同個名字等等狀況，看得懂在講什麼，但看起來真的很荒謬XD；玩一玩不知道觸動了什麼會大掉幀(遇過兩次)，打賭博公爵的時候也會莫名掉幀[spoiler]整個遊戲只有打他的時候會這樣，我還把所有能調的都調低再去打他[/spoiler]；明明是主線進度卻要靠賽刷到碎片才能推進；時至今日遊戲仍然會突然Error然後直接關掉......其實還有，一時想不起來，但想必這些已經夠多來說明這個遊戲有多神奇了。  雖然有一大堆小毛病存在，但遊戲玩起來真的停不下來，特別是煉出真真空刃之後，整天腦子裡都是真空刃的聲音。  遊戲內容很多時候都在刷怪，為了刷碎片或刷材料要拿去分解，特別是解食物的任務，東奔西跑東湊西湊就為了湊出個食物；藏了很多東西，連主線進度都給你藏進碎片裡，基本上不看線上的攻略會玩到不知道接下來要幹嘛，特別是真結局，由於探圖會很自然地打到吉貝爾，因為太好打了，打完看著game over一頭霧水，跑去查攻略，發現要打到真結局還要打很多東西。由於有打寶要素存在，常常會缺東西但又不知道哪裡有或誰會掉，如果不看攻略感覺好的話多花時間可以矇出來，壞的話就是土法煉鋼每隻都刷刷看[spoiler]然後發現藍寶箱裡有[/spoiler]。  總的來說，非常好玩，一定要玩，但你如果要全成就的話先考慮，因為之前多了一個DLC，算是另一個遊戲了，但成就併進來，所以就算全成就了，也會卡著那個DLC的成就，除非你買DLC並完成該DLC的全成就，才會真正地達成全成就。

Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night  -Rpg -Castlevania -Metroidvania -Action -Adventure -Loot -Interface e Legendas em PT/BR -Conquistas Steam    Como não expressar gratidão por esse incrível jogo que começou no Kickstarter e agora temos esse fantástico Castlevania 3D, mais modernizado em relação ao nosso querido SOTN? O Rpg é agradável e viciante, com uma história fascinante e interessante.   Além disso, oferece uma ampla variedade de armas, itens e pets (summons), tudo perfeitamente integrado ao jogo, sendo uma versão mais moderna de Castlevania.  Cenários deslumbrantes, itens ocultos, segredos e muita jogabilidade, de fato, essa versão para PC está incrível em comparação com a versão para SWITCH.   Aqui está um título para se jogar tranquilamente, acompanhando a história e desfrutando do clássico Castlevania.  Hoje em dia, temos vários títulos no mercado com semelhanças a Castlevania, mas muitos não chegam nem perto, por isso recomendo fortemente Bloodstained.  Bloodstained é praticamente considerado o sucessor espiritual do SOTN sem sombras de duvidas.

Серия самых коротких рецензий:  Отличная (8.5 из 10)  P.S.: Расшифровка:  Трэш — знакомиться с игрой рекомендуется исключительно тем, кто в диком восторге от описания, скриншотов и продолжительности. Остальным, из контакта с этой игрой, только тыкать палкой, как ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ку на дороге.  Так себе — в основном, эта игра предлагает меньше, чем вы увидите из описания и скриншотов. Рекомендуется только тем, кому вышеперечисленные критерии окажутся «зашибись».  Нормальная — неплохая игра своего жанра. Сами судите по описанию, скриншотам и продолжительности. Игра, примерно, соответствует своему наполнению.  Хорошая — весьма неплохой представитель своего жанра. Многие могут найти тут «свой шедевр». Зачастую, игра приятно соответствует своему описанию и скриншотам.  Отличная — эти игры, зачастую, превосходят свои первые впечатления, основанные на описании и скриншотах (в расчёт не берём репутацию, ибо «тот кто знает, тот поймёт»). Отличные представители своего жанра.

我非常推荐想玩类银河恶魔城游戏，但不知道从何下手的玩家来玩这一款游戏。这款游戏内似乎没有什么特别优秀的点，建模比较塑料、剧情也有些老套，但它就是非常好玩。 我玩过盗版的神之亵渎1&2，玩了 空洞骑士 和 空洞骑士：丝之歌。我能说的是，渎神1和空洞骑士的独特的美术风格和世界的构建给我带了深刻的印象，也非常好玩，但这款游戏blood stained，是更纯粹的快乐。 我习惯玩游戏时听点东西，比如直播或者长视频，但这款游戏甚至能让我忘记我可以听视频这件事。 如果非要说的话，这款游戏是丝之歌的完全反面，丝之歌拥有最好的纸面数据和一般（甚至是糟糕）的游戏体验，而这款游戏拥有一般的纸面数据，但玩起来非常棒！ 不过这款游戏的存档系统过于原始，不能自动存档，玩的时候一定要及时回存档点存档。希望在下一作中，制作者可以增加自动保存游戏进度的功能，把复活点和休息点变成单纯的复活和休息点，而不是存档点。
武器后摇长，手感不佳，画面又特色但有些复古，连招作用小，可以说几乎没有收益。但除此之外优点很多，地图设计很好，体量足，区域间衔接的好，BOSS设计各有特色。武器种类多，但操作不太灵活，在战斗体验上提升不大，有些中规中矩，隐藏区域和结局不错，探索体验方面很好，个人比较喜欢的一部银河城作品。
Прекрасная метроидвания, любителям советую.

Pretty fun game.  Although, it kind of seems like they made way too much to pack into it and then didn't smooth it out and optimize it to have a steady flow throughout the game.  Graphics are great.  Music is a bit repetitive.  Sound is good, but sometimes a bit cheesy.  Insane amounts of items.  This is a pro and a con.  Crafting it fun and grinding for comps is mindless fun.  However, 99% of the weapons and items in the game you will never use.  The 8 bit weapons easily destroy the whole point of most of the crating until end game.  They should have put in an independent crafting arc.  Many of the monsters seem just out of place where it seemed like they made so many monster models with big plans, that in the end they just stuck them in without any plans.  Oh, and then end game, with the 32 bit weapons, you can easily beat the boss...  Oh, and then the bosses.  Basically, you just stand and button mash.  I literally never had to learn a boss pattern.  Any completionist will easily breeze through the bosses.  The shard abilities are also largely pointless.  You will end up using like 3.  Again, there is so much lost in the lack of gradually progression with all the abilities and items in the game.  This goes with the bosses, too.  They are few and far between and although they are graphically cool, the events are, well uneventful.  Lastly, the in-game nods to the backers gets old; especially with all the cats.  Yeah, cat pictures and even giant cats.  Not like cool jaguars, no crazy cat lady house cats to be "cute."  The shout outs to large backers with the portraits on the walls were passable and thoughtful though.  There is a lot of extra touches the developers added that just get lost like if you have the fairy up and stand around too long, she will land and sit on your shoulder.  Definitely worth a weekend if you can get it for like $15 or less.

It didn't start on the right foot for me. The first and most notable factor are the graphics, which I'd summarize as "gaudy": lighting, textures, effects, and colors combine to form a very plastic look. Some locations are really well designed, if not relatively impressive, but most are unimaginative, scarcely detailed, and at times outright ugly; sadly, the last two areas of the game are among the worst-looking. Besides the overdesigned heroine, human characters aren't much better and suffer from ridiculous walking and running cycles. And while clearly the game doesn't take itself seriously, several enemies are really too ridiculous. Going past the visuals, I still wasn't impressed. It controls extremely well but starts with a feeling of being constantly too underpowered for your own good, while the abilities at hand remain limited.  But then, after taking some time to deliver the first important abilities and accumulating the first shards from enemies, it clicks. Offensive capabilities start to pile up, and I realized some restraint was needed to keep players from becoming too powerful down the line. The map reveals itself in its grand size; I lost count of how many times I thought I was at a good point with exploration only for another large area to be revealed. "A few rooms more" comes kicking in your head, and before you know it, hours have passed. The art direction doesn't get any better, but the soundtrack, which also features veteran Michiru Yamane, brings you back to the times of [b]Castlevania: Symphony of the Night[/b], as intended.  The game is packed with content and secrets to find, big and small, at the limit of the superfluous (is there something really useful about helping that villager grow crops?). Even if you clear almost all the map, even if you get the best ending, there will still be a lot to discover or play again, be it further exploration before the final battle, an always welcome New Game+, the short and silly Classic Mode, the Zangetsu Mode (which is more or less an equivalent of the Richter Mode), or... Aurora from [b]Child of Light[/b]?! Oh well, it's not like Ubisoft will use her again; it's a pretty fun variation on the regular gameplay, although I wish it had a little more story and cutscenes.  [b]Ritual of the Night[/b] is not the big masterpiece I hoped for it to be; I think in some aspects it has privileged quantity over quality, even if the quality is still good and even great in some parts, and could have used a better, more distinctive look. But it succeeds in its primary goals: being a spiritual successor to [b]SOTN[/b] and roping the player in for hours. They also have kept supporting it, with the latest DLC published a full 5 years after the base game. It's unlikely we'll see a sequel for a long time, as the 2026 [b]Bloodstained[/b] will be a prequel. But if and when it comes, hopefully they will have treasured all that went right here.

My only experience with Koji Igarashi's work is Bloodstained. I've appreciated Castlevania from a distance, and I've played the Lords of Shadow games, but I think it's time I fix that after playing this. This is far from a perfect game, but it's just a damned good one. The people who made it clearly have a passion for the genre, and Iga clearly loves his work. I could complain about the drop rates, the annoying enemy placement, the worthless dodge, the unresponsive fighting game special inputs, the fact that enemies hurt you just by touching you and most of your weapons are melee, and those are all worthy of complaint. But I can still tell you to pick this game up with my full chest. It's nice to play a game that's actually worth playing from a Kickstarter. It's nice to play a game where the main character is a conventionally attractive female that DOESN'T hate men or belittle them simply by being stronger than them. It's nice to play a game where the people who hate God are the bad guys. It's nice to play a game where the lines between good and evil are clearly defined and we're playing as the good guys (okay, there is content where you get to play as the bad guys which is cool, but my point stands). It's nice for a game to randomly get free updates with new content. It's nice to know a pillar of gaming lives on in everything but name and that this whole Metroidvania renaissance was helmed by one of the guys that popularized the genre. I have paid little attention to the buzz regarding the sequel, and I likely wouldn't be playing it at launch regardless, but I'm hopeful that the devs can take what they made here and surpass it.

Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night is a solid Igavania, crafted by the man himself, and it wears that legacy proudly. The map design, soundtrack, and progression all hit the right nostalgic notes, and it absolutely feels like the spiritual successor to Symphony of the Night we’ve been begging for. The problem is, it just doesn’t quite sing like the old days.  Miriam is fine, but she’s no Alucard. The story is campy even by Castlevania standards, the dialogue feels like it was written during a sugar rush, and the game has more jank than a flea market laptop. It’s fun, don’t get me wrong, but between the floaty combat, weird pacing, and occasionally ugly visuals, it’s hard not to wish it had a little more polish and soul.  As for people whining that it’s too easy, that’s on you. Nobody’s forcing you to use the busted shards or the overpowered weapons. You made your own easy mode. Go swing a knife and suffer like the rest of us.  Despite the rough edges, it’s a damn respectable effort. It’s not Symphony of the Night 2, but it is a decent "Spiritual Successor" (God they loved that term in the Peak Kickstarter Era, didn't they?) closer to the DS Castlevanias, maybe even a bit below them. Still, it’s stylish, playable, and dripping with charm where it counts.  Verdict: Janky, and occasionally goofy, but still worth playing for diehard Castlevania fans.  7.5/10  not quite a symphony, but definitely a solid composition (although perhaps not as much of an earworm as we had hoped after so long.)

◯悪魔城ドラキュラシリーズのお義父さんIGAが作った、悪魔城ライクなゲーム。 版権の都合で悪魔城ではないが、作品の魂がもう、そのものである。 シリアスなストーリー、ビジュアルであるが、実際にプレイするとコテコテのパロディに溢れており、縁もゆかりも無い他作品のネタをぶち込んでくる。 装備品が混沌の極みであり、プレイヤー側がネタに振るまでもなく、ヒロインは奇天烈なコスプレで戦うハメになる。まともな格好をすることのほうが縛りプレイの様相を呈している。 声優への演技指導も悪ノリするときがあり、 [h1]ぶっ殺しておくれぇぇ!!!!![/h1] などといった怪演が、シリアスな空気の中において唐突に殴りつけてきたりする。 このノリを是とするか非とするかで評価は変わるだろう。  ◯ゲームバランスは素晴らしい。探索系アクションーーメトロイドヴァニアでも上澄み。ヴァニア側のお父さんだもの。それはそう。 好み分かれそうな要素としては、稼ぎの機会が多いところ。 各敵にはスキル、アイテムのドロップ率が設定されており、４％のアイテムを狙うとなればけっこう狩らなければならない。 敵は画面を切り替えるだけで、読み込みを挟むこともなく即リスポンする。サクサク気持ちよく狩れるが稼ぎは稼ぎだ。出なさすぎて狼狽することも多い。 一例：[spoiler]クレープ食べたいお婆ちゃんのために、死んだ魚の目でドラゴン狩りをするターンとかある。[/spoiler]  ◯開発資金をキックスターターで集めた経緯があり、支援者への返礼が本編中に散りばめられている。 現代的な顔をした肖像画がやたら多いのがソレ。他、可愛い犬猫が出てくるのもソレ。 意味深な部屋が何かと思ったら、支援者へのお礼の部屋だったりする。開発を支えてくれた感謝はあれど正直紛らわしい。  ◯他作品とのコラボが多い。 クリア後にプレイアブル解禁なやつは良いんだけど、一周目からシャンティのコラボ装備を所持しているのはさすがにやめてほしかった。  ◯現在の値段に対して、破格の内容なので、興味があるならぜひ購入して、売上に貢献して欲しい。 じきに続編が出るぞ。買え、買うんだ！
